---
title: "ManuscriptOutput"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-28"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = F, message = F)
library(tidyverse)
library(vegan)
library(stats)
library(data.table)
library(lubridate)
library(dplyr)
library(factoextra)
library(multcompView)
library(NADA)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
library(randomForest)
library(scales)
library(ggnewscale)
options(ggrepel.max.overlaps=Inf)
```

```{r functions and constants}
bugSites = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
             "Superior Associates",
             "Highway 36",
             "CTC Open Space")

'%!in%' <- function(x,y)!('%in%'(x,y))

dateFormat = "%b"

dateX = scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                     date_breaks = '1 month')

month_one = scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                         date_breaks = '1 month')

month_two = scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                         date_breaks = '2 month')

month_three = scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                         date_breaks = '3 month')

lm_eqn <- function(df){
    m <- lm(y ~ x, df);
    pv = with(summary(m), pf(fstatistic[1],fstatistic[2],fstatistic[3],lower.tail=F));
    eq <- substitute(italic(y) == a + b %.% italic(x)*
                       ","~~italic(r)^2~"="~r2,
                       #","~~italic(p)*"="~pvn, 
         list(a = format(unname(coef(m)[1]), digits = 2),
              b = format(unname(coef(m)[2]), digits = 2),
             r2 = format(summary(m)$r.squared, digits = 3)))#,
            # pvn = format(unname(pv),digits = 3)))
#             pv = formatC(pv,format="e",digits=1)))
    as.character(as.expression(eq));
}

lm_eqn2 <- function(df){
    m <- lm(y ~ x, df);
    pv = with(summary(m), pf(fstatistic[1],fstatistic[2],fstatistic[3],lower.tail=F));
    eq <- substitute(italic(p)*"="~pvn, 
         list(#a = format(unname(coef(m)[1]), digits = 2),
              #b = format(unname(coef(m)[2]), digits = 2),
             #r2 = format(summary(m)$r.squared, digits = 3),
             pvn = format(unname(pv),digits = 3)))
#             pv = formatC(pv,format="e",digits=1)))
    as.character(as.expression(eq));
}

cbLookup_message <- c("Greenbelt Plateau" = "#999999",
              "Superior Associates" = "#479f74",
              "Highway 36" ="#56b4e9",
              "Dutch Creek" ="#f0e34b",
              "CTC Open Space"= "#3172b2",
              "Mulberry" = "#000000")
cbLookup = cbLookup_message

cbLookupBug = cbLookup[c(1,2,3,5)]
shapesBug = c(15, 16, 17, 18)
shapes = c(15, 16, 17, 25, 18, 16)

shapeLookup = c("Greenbelt Plateau" = 19,
              "Superior Associates" = 15,
              "Highway 36" = 24,
              "Dutch Creek" = 23,
              "CTC Open Space"= 25,
              "Mulberry" = 19)

shapeLookupPartic = c("Greenbelt Plateau" = 19,
              "Superior Associates" = 15,
              "Highway 36" = 24,
              "Dutch Creek Park" = 23,
              "CTC Open Space"= 25,
              "Mulberry" = 19)

tri.to.squ<-function(x)
{
  rn<-row.names(x)
  cn<-colnames(x)
  an<-unique(c(cn,rn))
  myval<-x[!is.na(x)]
  mymat<-matrix(1,nrow=length(an),ncol=length(an),dimnames=list(an,an))
  for(ext in 1:length(cn))
  {
    for(int in 1:length(rn))
    {
      if(is.na(x[row.names(x)==rn[int],colnames(x)==cn[ext]])) next
      mymat[row.names(mymat)==rn[int],colnames(mymat)==cn[ext]]<-x[row.names(x)==rn[int],colnames(x)==cn[ext]]
      mymat[row.names(mymat)==cn[ext],colnames(mymat)==rn[int]]<-x[row.names(x)==rn[int],colnames(x)==cn[ext]]
    }
    
  }
  return(mymat)
}

wilcox_tibble = function(dataIn = data_all,
                         var = c('')){
  dat = dataIn %>%
    select(Site,all_of(var))
  
  wilcox_result = pairwise.wilcox.test(dat %>% pull(var),
                                         dat %>% pull(Site),
                                         p.adjust.method = 'none')
    
    # Check that there was enough data to run a wilcox test. If the pvalue 
    # matrix has 0 rows, then set 'pValue' to False so the labels get set 
    # correctly later.
    if(nrow(wilcox_result$p.value) == 0){
      return(0)
    } else {
      # Use a  function to turn the triangle matrix into a square matrix 
      wilcox_matr = tri.to.squ(wilcox_result$p.value)
      
      # Use a function to turn the matrix of p-values into a list of letters 
      # for each site.Each site is given a letter based on the p-value 
      # relationships with each other site. If a p-value between sites
      # is less than or equal to 0.05, they will be marked as
      # different groups.
      let = multcompLetters(wilcox_matr,
                            compare="<=",
                            threshold = 0.05,
                            Letters = letters)
    }
  output = tibble(Sites = names(let$Letters),
                  Letters = let$Letters,
                  Variable = var) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Sites,
                values_from = Letters)
  
  datN = dat %>% group_by(Site) %>% summarise(n=n()) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = 'Site', values_from = n) 
  
  names(datN) = paste0(names(datN),"_n")
  
  output = bind_cols(output,datN)
  
  return(output)
}

txtSize = 26
pltSize = 26
numSize = 20

bottomL = theme(text=element_text(size=pltSize),
      legend.position='bottom',
      panel.grid.major.y=element_line(color='grey'),
      panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_blank(),
      axis.line=element_line(colour='black'),
      axis.text=element_text(size=numSize),
      legend.key=element_blank())

blankTheme = theme(text=element_text(size=pltSize),
      panel.grid.major.y=element_line(color='grey'),
      panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_blank(),
      axis.text=element_text(size=numSize),
      axis.line=element_line(colour='black'),
      legend.key=element_blank())

bottomSlant = bottomL +
  #theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=0.5,vjust=0.5))
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1,vjust=1))

siteOrder = c("Greenbelt Plateau","Superior Associates",
              "Highway 36","Dutch Creek Park",
              "CTC Open Space", "Mulberry")

make_overtime_censored = function(data,analyte,cenCol,xR,txtSize=24,al=.5){
  if(al != 1){
    lt = 1
  } else {
    lt = 2
  }

  metHere = analyte#setdiff(metNames,setdiff(metNames,names(data)))
  cenHere = cenCol#paste0(metHere,"Cen")

  data = data %>%
    filter(!is.na(get(metHere)))
    
  vals = data %>%
    select(Date, Site, all_of(metHere)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -c(Date,Site),
                 names_to = "Variable",
                 values_to = "Value")
  
  maxV = data %>%
    pull(all_of(metHere)) %>%
    max()
  
  maxY = maxV * 1.2

  cenVals = data %>%
    select(Date, Site, all_of(cenHere)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -c(Date,Site),
                 names_to = "Variable",
                 values_to = "ValueCen") %>%
    mutate(
      ValueCen = case_when(
        ValueCen == "0" ~ F,
        T ~ T),
      Variable = sub("Cen","",Variable))
  
  
  longCen = vals %>%
    full_join(cenVals, by = c("Date","Site","Variable")) %>%
    mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                          "Superior Associates",
                                          "Highway 36",
                                          "Dutch Creek Park",
                                          "CTC Open Space",
                                          "Mulberry")))

  longCen = longCen %>% left_join(data %>% select(Date, Site, Colr, Shp),
                                  by = c("Date","Site"))
  
  pltPoint = longCen %>%
    filter(!ValueCen)
  
  pltLine = longCen %>%
    filter(ValueCen)
  
  pltLine = pltLine  %>%
    mutate(DateDif = case_when(
      Site == "Greenbelt Plateau" ~ 0.6,
      Site == "Superior Associates" ~ 0.2,
      Site == "Highway 36" ~ 0.4,
      Site == "Dutch Creek Park" ~ 0,
      Site == "CTC Open Space" ~ 0.8,
      Site == "Mulberry" ~ 0
    ))
  
  plt = ggplot() +
    bottomSlant +
    geom_point(data = pltPoint,
               color = pltPoint$Colr,
               shape = pltPoint$Shp,
               fill = pltPoint$Colr,
               aes(x = Date, y = Value),
               size = 6)
  if(metHere %in% c("Na_Filtered","Sr_Filtered","Na_Total","Sr_Total","Chloride","Sulfate")){
    plt = plt + 
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date+DateDif, xend = Date+DateDif,
                     y = Value, yend = maxY),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date-1+DateDif, xend = Date+1+DateDif,
                     y = Value, yend = Value),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                 date_breaks = '1 month',
                 limits = xR) + 
    labs(shape = "", color = "", linetype = "", x = "", y = "") +
    theme(legend.position = 'none',
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          text = element_text(size=txtSize)) +
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,maxY),
                         expand = c(0,0),
                         labels = label_comma())
  } else {
    plt = plt + 
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date+DateDif, xend = Date+DateDif,
                     y = 0, yend = Value),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date-1+DateDif, xend = Date+1+DateDif,
                     y = Value, yend = Value),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date-1+DateDif, xend = Date+1+DateDif,
                     y = 0, yend = 0),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    #scale_color_manual(values = colLst) +
    #scale_fill_manual(values = colLst) +
    #scale_shape_manual(values = shps) +
    scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                 date_breaks = '1 month',
                 limits = xR) +
    labs(shape = "", color = "", linetype = "", x = "", y = "") +
    theme(legend.position = 'none',
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          text = element_text(size=txtSize)) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma())
  }
  plt
}

make_overtime_censored_both = function(dataIn,analyte,cenCol,xR,particData,txtSize=24,al=.5){
  if(al != 1){
    lt = 1
  } else {
    lt = 2
  }

  metHere = analyte#setdiff(metNames,setdiff(metNames,names(data)))
  cenHere = cenCol#paste0(metHere,"Cen")
  metOnly = sub("_.*","",metHere)

  data = dataIn %>%
    mutate(Colr = cbLookupParticFilt[Site]) %>%
    filter(!is.na(get(metHere)))
    
  vals = data %>%
    select(Date, Site, all_of(metHere)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -c(Date,Site),
                 names_to = "Variable",
                 values_to = "Value")
  
  maxV = data %>%
    pull(all_of(metHere)) %>%
    max()
  
  maxY = maxV * 1.2

  cenVals = data %>%
    select(Date, Site, all_of(cenHere)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -c(Date,Site),
                 names_to = "Variable",
                 values_to = "ValueCen") %>%
    mutate(
      ValueCen = case_when(
        ValueCen == "0" ~ F,
        T ~ T),
      Variable = sub("Cen","",Variable))
  
  
  longCen = vals %>%
    full_join(cenVals, by = c("Date","Site","Variable")) %>%
    mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                          "Superior Associates",
                                          "Highway 36",
                                          "Dutch Creek Park",
                                          "CTC Open Space",
                                          "Mulberry")))

  longCen = longCen %>% left_join(data %>% select(Date, Site, Colr, Shp),
                                  by = c("Date","Site"))
  
  particData = particData %>%
    mutate(Colr = cbLookupPartic[Site],
         Shp = shapeLookup[Site])
  
  particPoint = particData %>%
    filter(Metal == metOnly,
           ParticulateMin == ParticulateMax)
  
  particLine = particData %>%
    filter(Metal == metOnly,
           ParticulateMin != ParticulateMax)
  
  pltPoint = longCen %>%
    filter(!ValueCen)
  
  pltLine = longCen %>%
    filter(ValueCen)
  
  pltLine = pltLine  %>%
    mutate(DateDif = case_when(
      Site == "Greenbelt Plateau" ~ 0.6,
      Site == "Superior Associates" ~ 0.2,
      Site == "Highway 36" ~ 0.4,
      Site == "Dutch Creek Park" ~ 0,
      Site == "CTC Open Space" ~ 0.8,
      Site == "Mulberry" ~ 0
    ))
  
  plt = ggplot() +
    bottomSlant +
    geom_point(data = pltPoint,
               color = pltPoint$Colr,
               shape = pltPoint$Shp,
               fill = pltPoint$Colr,
               aes(x = Date, y = Value),
               size = 6)
  if(metHere %in% c("Na_Filtered","Sr_Filtered","Na_Total","Sr_Total")){
    plt = plt + 
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date+DateDif, xend = Date+DateDif,
                     y = Value, yend = maxY),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date-1+DateDif, xend = Date+1+DateDif,
                     y = Value, yend = Value),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                 date_breaks = '1 month',
                 limits = xR) + 
    labs(shape = "", color = "", linetype = "", x = "", y = "") +
    theme(legend.position = 'none',
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          text = element_text(size=txtSize)) +
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,maxY),
                         expand = c(0,0),
                         labels = label_comma())
  } else {
    plt = plt + 
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date+DateDif, xend = Date+DateDif,
                     y = 0, yend = Value),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date-1+DateDif, xend = Date+1+DateDif,
                     y = Value, yend = Value),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    geom_segment(data = pltLine,
                 color = pltLine$Colr,
                 aes(x = Date-1+DateDif, xend = Date+1+DateDif,
                     y = 0, yend = 0),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = lt,
                 alpha = al) +
    #scale_color_manual(values = colLst) +
    #scale_fill_manual(values = colLst) +
    #scale_shape_manual(values = shps) +
    scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                 date_breaks = '1 month',
                 limits = xR) +
    labs(shape = "", color = "", linetype = "", x = "", y = "") +
    theme(legend.position = 'none',
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          text = element_text(size=txtSize)) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma())
  }
  
  plt = plt +
    geom_point(data = particPoint,
               color = particPoint$Colr,
               shape = particPoint$Shp,
               fill = particPoint$Colr,
               size = 6,
               aes(x=Date, y=ParticulateMax))
  
  plt
}

regimeGrid = function(data, analytes, censors){
  outPlts = vector(mode = 'list', length = 3*length(analytes))
  sm = data %>% filter(Type == "Snowmelt")
  smX = sm$Date %>% range()
  smX = as.Date(c("2022-01-01","2022-11-29"))
  rn = data %>% filter(Type == "Rain")
  rnX = rn$Date %>% range()
  rnX = as.Date(c("2022-01-01","2022-11-29"))
  bf = data %>% filter(Type == "Baseflow")
  bfX = bf$Date %>% range()
  bfX = as.Date(c("2022-01-01","2022-11-29"))
  for(i in 1:length(analytes)){
    yLab = val_cen_lab %>% filter(Name == analytes[i]) %>% pull(Label)
    #data = dataIn %>% filter(!is.na(analytes[i]))
    pltSnow = make_overtime_censored(sm, analytes[i], censors[i],smX) 
    if(yLab %in% mgs){
      pltSnow = pltSnow + labs(y=bquote(.(yLab)*" (mg L"^-1*")"))
    } else if(yLab == "Ammonia"){
      pltSnow = pltSnow + labs(y=bquote("Ammonium (μg L"^-1*")"))
    } else if(yLab == "Conductivity"){
      pltSnow = pltSnow + labs(y=bquote(.(yLab)*" (μS cm"^-1*")"))
    } else if(yLab %in% c("Alkalinity","Hardness")){
      pltSnow = pltSnow + labs(y=bquote(.(yLab)*" (mg L"^-1*")"))
    } else if(yLab %!in% no_bq){
      pltSnow = pltSnow + labs(y=bquote(.(yLab)*" (μg L"^-1*")"))
    } else {
      pltSnow = pltSnow + labs(y=yLab)
    }
    pltRain = make_overtime_censored(rn, analytes[i],censors[i],rnX)
    pltBase = make_overtime_censored(bf, analytes[i],censors[i],bfX)
    outPlts[[i*3-2]] = pltSnow
    outPlts[[i*3-1]] = pltRain
    outPlts[[i*3]] = pltBase
    if(analytes[i]=="TDP"){
      outPlts[[i*3-2]] = pltSnow + 
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1,vjust=1))
    }
  }
  outPlts[[1]] = outPlts[[1]] + labs(title = "Snowmelt") + 
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
  outPlts[[2]] = outPlts[[2]] + labs(title = "Rain") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
  outPlts[[3]] = outPlts[[3]] + labs(title = "Baseflow") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
  outPlts[[i*3-2]] = outPlts[[i*3-2]] +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1,vjust=1))
  outPlts[[i*3-1]] = outPlts[[i*3-1]] +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1,vjust=1))
  outPlts[[i*3-0]] = outPlts[[i*3-0]] +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1,vjust=1))
  outPlts
}

RegimeSave = function(analytes,name,data=wide4,
                      pltPth = ""){
  jHs = c(7,11,15,20,25,30,35,40,45,50,55,60)
  jpgH = jHs[length(analytes)]
  nms = val_cen_lab %>% 
    filter(Name %in% analytes) %>%
    pull(Name)
  cens = val_cen_lab %>%
    filter(Name %in% analytes) %>%
    pull(CenN)
  plts = regimeGrid(data, nms, cens)
  plts_grid = plot_grid(plotlist = plts, ncol = 3, align = 'v')
  ggsave(paste0(pltPth,name,".jpg"),
         plts_grid,
         device = 'jpg',
         width = 23,
         height = jpgH)
}


regime_boxplot_many = function(datIn, varCen, fName='',wilcs=wilcVals,
                          pth = "./",sameScale = F,
                          saveImg = F){
  pList = vector(mode = 'list', length = 3*length(varCen))
  i = 1
  for(var in varCen){
    varMaxY = 0
    pt = make_boxplot_censored_1yrLet_Reg(datIn,var,wilcs,"Snowmelt")
    if(i<(length(pList)-2)){pt = pt + labs(x='')}
    pList[[i]] = pt
    maxY =layer_scales(pt)$y$range$range[2]
    if(maxY > varMaxY){varMaxY = maxY}
    i = i + 1
    
    pt = make_boxplot_censored_1yrLet_Reg(datIn,var,wilcs,"Rain")
    if(i<(length(pList)-2)){pt = pt + labs(x='')}
    pt = pt + labs(y='')
    pList[[i]] = pt
    maxY =layer_scales(pt)$y$range$range[2]
    if(maxY > varMaxY){varMaxY = maxY}
    i = i + 1
    
    pt = make_boxplot_censored_1yrLet_Reg(datIn,var,wilcs,"Baseflow")
    if(i<(length(pList)-2)){pt = pt + labs(x='')}
    pt = pt + labs(y='')
    pList[[i]] = pt
    maxY =layer_scales(pt)$y$range$range[2]
    if(maxY > varMaxY){varMaxY = maxY}
    i = i + 1
    
    if(sameScale){
      pList[[i-1]] = pList[[i-1]] + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(NA,varMaxY))
      pList[[i-2]] = pList[[i-2]] + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(NA,varMaxY))
      pList[[i-3]] = pList[[i-3]] + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(NA,varMaxY))
    }
  }
  pList[[i-1]] = pList[[i-1]] + theme(axis.text.x = element_text())
  pList[[i-2]] = pList[[i-2]] + theme(axis.text.x = element_text())
  pList[[i-3]] = pList[[i-3]] + theme(axis.text.x = element_text())
  pOut = plot_grid(plotlist = pList,align = 'vh',ncol=3)
  #name = sub("Cen","",varCen)
  if(saveImg){
    ggsave(paste0(pth,fName,'.jpg'),
           pOut,
           device = 'jpg',
           width = 23,
           height = 5*length(varCen)+2)
  }
}

make_boxplot_censored_1yrLet_Reg = function(dataIn,cenName,
                                            wilcLet,regime,
                                            txtSize = 24,
                                            doCen=T){
  
  met = sub("Cen","",cenName )
  data = dataIn %>%
    filter(Type == regime) %>%
    mutate(Site = factor(Site,
                         levels = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                    "Superior Associates",
                                    "Highway 36",
                                    #"Dutch Creek Park",
                                    "CTC Open Space")),
                                    #"Mulberry")),
           Site2 = case_when(
             Site == "Greenbelt Plateau" ~ "Greenbelt\nPlateau",
             Site == "Superior Associates" ~ "Superior\nAssociates",
             #Site == "Dutch Creek Park" ~ "Dutch Creek\nPark",
             Site == "CTC Open Space" ~ "CTC Open\nSpace",
             T ~ Site),
           Site2 = factor(Site2,
                          levels = c("Greenbelt\nPlateau",
                                     "Superior\nAssociates",
                                     "Highway 36",
                                     #"Dutch Creek\nPark",
                                     "CTC Open\nSpace"))) %>%
                                     #"Mulberry"))) %>%
    select(Site, all_of(c(met,cenName)),Colr,Site2)
  
  siteOrder = data$Site %>% levels()
  
#  if(met == "Fe" & tvd == "Total"){
#    data = data %>%
#      filter(Fe < 30000)
#  }
  
  cenPeak0 = data %>%
    select(Site) %>%
    unique() %>%
    arrange(Site) %>%
    mutate(max0 = 0)
  
  measMinInf = data %>%
    select(Site) %>%
    unique() %>%
    arrange(Site) %>%
    mutate(minInf = Inf)
  
  dataCenPeakVals = data %>%
    arrange(Site) %>%
    mutate(Val = .[[2]],
           Cen = .[[3]]) %>%
    filter(Cen != "0") %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    mutate(max = max(Val,na.rm=T),
           min = min(Val,na.rm=T)) %>%
    select(Site,max,min) %>%
    unique()
  
  dataMeasMinVals = data %>%
    arrange(Site) %>%
    mutate(Val = .[[2]],
           Cen = .[[3]]) %>%
    filter(Cen == "0") %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    mutate(#max = max(Val),
           min = min(Val)) %>%
    select(Site,min) %>%
    unique()
  
  dataNums = data %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    summarise(n=n())
  
  dataMeasMaxVals = data %>%
    arrange(Site) %>%
    mutate(Val = .[[2]],
           Cen = .[[3]]) %>%
    filter(Cen == "0") %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    mutate(#max = max(Val),
           max = max(Val,na.rm=T)) %>%
    select(Site,max) %>%
    unique()
  
  dataCenPeaks = left_join(cenPeak0,
                           dataCenPeakVals) %>%
    mutate(
      max = case_when(
        is.na(max) ~ 0,
        T ~ max),
      min = case_when(
        is.na(min) ~ 0,
        T ~ min
      )) %>%
    select(-max0)
  
  dataMeasMins = left_join(measMinInf,
                           dataMeasMinVals) %>%
    mutate(
      #max = case_when(
      #  is.na(max) ~ 0,
      #  T ~ max),
      min = case_when(
        is.na(min) ~ Inf,
        T ~ min
      )) %>%
    select(-minInf)
  
  
  maxs = dataCenPeaks %>% pull(max)
  lows = dataCenPeaks %>% pull(min)
  mins = rep(0,length(maxs))
  minMeas = dataMeasMins %>% pull(min)
  maxMeas = dataMeasMaxVals %>% pull(max)
  
  
  if(!(met == "Na_Total") & 
     !(met == "Na_Filtered") &
     !(met == "Chloride") &
     !(met == "Sulfate")){
  for(i in 1:length(lows)){
    if(minMeas[i] < lows[i]){
      # multiply censored, with one value below the lowest measured, so set
      # lowest MRL to 0, so full area becomes light grey covered.
      lows[i] = 0
    }
  }
  }
  
  
  
  x_strt = c()
  x_end = c()
  for(i in 1:nrow(dataCenPeaks)){
    x_strt = c(x_strt,-.5+i)
    x_end = c(x_end,.5+i)
  }
  
  dataPlt = data %>%
    mutate(Val = .[[2]])
  
  for(i in 1:length(maxs)){
    ste = siteOrder[i]
    if(maxs[i] == lows[i]){
      # Runs if this site/analyte pair is singularly censored. Replaces
      # values at the MRL with 90% of that value, to ensure it is below
      # the censored area.
      dataPlt = dataPlt %>%
        mutate(Val = case_when(
          (Site == ste & Val == maxs[i]) ~ 0.9*maxs[i],
          T ~ Val))
    }
  }
  
  maxAll = dataPlt %>% pull(Val) %>% max(na.rm=T)
  
  if(met %in% c("Na_Total","Na_Filtered")){
    lowOrig = lows
    maxOrig = maxs
    # Min to Low is solid
    # Min to Max is alpha

    # Now Min is maxAll
    mins = rep(maxAll,nrow(dataCenPeaks))
    lows = maxOrig
    lows[lows == 0] = maxAll
    maxs = lowOrig
    maxs[maxs == 0] = maxAll
    
    lows[4] = maxAll

    
  } else if (met %in% c("Chloride","Sulfate")){
    for(i in 1:4){
      if(maxs[i] == 0){
        maxs[i] = 0
        mins[i] = 0
        lows[i] = 0
      } else if(maxMeas[i] >= lows[i]){
      # There was measured value above highest censored, so all light grey
        maxs[i] = lows[i]
        mins[i] = maxAll
        lows[i] = maxAll
      } else {
        mins[i] = maxAll
        maxs[i] = maxAll
      }
    }
  } else if(met == "Sr_Total"){
    mins[1] = maxAll
    maxs[1] = 500
    lows[1] = maxAll
  }

  
  fillColr = dataPlt %>%
    select(Site2,Colr) %>%
    unique() %>%
    arrange(Site2) %>%
    pull(Colr) %>%
    as.vector()
  
  fillColrB = dataPlt %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    mutate(n=n()) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(n>3) %>%
    select(Site2,Colr) %>%
    unique() %>%
    arrange(Site2) %>%
    pull(Colr) %>%
    as.vector
  
  fillColrP = dataPlt %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    mutate(n=n()) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(n<=3) %>%
    select(Site2,Colr) %>%
    unique() %>%
    arrange(Site2) %>%
    pull(Colr) %>%
    as.vector
  
  wilcLets = wilcLet %>%
    filter(FlowRegime == regime) %>%
    #filter(is.na(FlowRegime)) %>%
    filter(Analyte == met) %>%
    select(all_of(siteOrder)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
                 names_to ="Site",
                 values_to = "Let")
  
  wilcX = (x_end - x_strt)/2 + x_strt
  
  wilcLoc = dataPlt %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    summarise(m = max(get(met),na.rm=T)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(maxV = max(m),
           m = case_when(
             met == 'pH' ~ 6,
             T ~ m + maxV*.1)) %>%
    select(-maxV) %>%
    mutate(xLoc = wilcX) %>%
    left_join(wilcLets)
  
  
  numLoc = wilcLoc %>%
    left_join(dataNums) %>%
    mutate(y_num = min(0-m/15))
  
#  if(met == "Al_Filtered"){
#    wilcLoc = wilcLoc %>%
#      mutate(m = case_when(
#        Site == "Superior Associates" ~ 150,
#        T ~ m))
#  }
  
  
#  tvlab = sub(".*_","",met)
#  mlab =  sub("_.*","",met)

  
  if(min(numLoc$n) <= 3){
    dataPlt = dataPlt %>%
      mutate(negY = -200) %>%
      group_by(Site) %>%
      mutate(n=n())
    
    pt = ggplot() +
      geom_point(data = dataPlt, aes(x=Site2, y = negY)) +
      geom_boxplot(data = dataPlt %>% filter(n > 3),
                   aes(x=Site2, y = Val, fill = Site2)) +
      scale_fill_manual(values=fillColrB) +
      new_scale_fill()+
      geom_point(data = dataPlt %>% filter(n<=3),
                 aes(x=Site2, y = Val,color = Site2,fill = Site2),
                 size = 4)+
      scale_color_manual(values = fillColrP) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = fillColrP) +
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,NA)) +
      labs(fill = '',color = '')

  } else {
    pt = ggplot() +
      geom_boxplot(data = dataPlt,
                   aes(y = Val,
                       x = Site2,
                       fill = Site2)) +
          scale_fill_manual(values=fillColr)
  }
  if(doCen){
  pt = pt +
    # Light grey for the max MRL
    geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[1],xmax=x_end[1],ymin=mins[1],ymax=maxs[1]),alpha=.5) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[2],xmax=x_end[2],ymin=mins[2],ymax=maxs[2]),alpha=.5) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[3],xmax=x_end[3],ymin=mins[3],ymax=maxs[3]),alpha=.5) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[4],xmax=x_end[4],ymin=mins[4],ymax=maxs[4]),alpha=.5) +
    #geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[5],xmax=x_end[5],ymin=mins[5],ymax=maxs[5]),alpha=.5) +
    #geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[6],xmax=x_end[6],ymin=mins[6],ymax=maxs[6]),alpha=.5) +
    # Solid grey for lowest MRL
    geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[1],xmax=x_end[1],ymin=mins[1],ymax=lows[1])) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[2],xmax=x_end[2],ymin=mins[2],ymax=lows[2])) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[3],xmax=x_end[3],ymin=mins[3],ymax=lows[3])) +
    geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[4],xmax=x_end[4],ymin=mins[4],ymax=lows[4])) +
    #geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[5],xmax=x_end[5],ymin=mins[5],ymax=lows[5])) +
    #geom_rect(aes(xmin=x_strt[6],xmax=x_end[6],ymin=mins[6],ymax=lows[6])) +
    
    geom_segment(aes(x=x_strt[1],xend=x_end[1],y=maxs[1],yend=maxs[1])) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=x_strt[2],xend=x_end[2],y=maxs[2],yend=maxs[2])) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=x_strt[3],xend=x_end[3],y=maxs[3],yend=maxs[3])) +
    geom_segment(aes(x=x_strt[4],xend=x_end[4],y=maxs[4],yend=maxs[4]))
  }
  pt = pt +
    #geom_segment(aes(x=x_strt[5],xend=x_end[5],y=maxs[5],yend=maxs[5])) +
    #geom_segment(aes(x=x_strt[6],xend=x_end[6],y=maxs[6],yend=maxs[6])) +

    geom_text(data = wilcLoc, aes(x=xLoc, y = m, label = Let),
              size = 6) +
    geom_text(data = numLoc, aes(x=xLoc,y=y_num,label = n),
              size = 6)+
    
    bottomSlant +
    labs(x=regime) + 
#    labs(y=bquote(.(tvlab)~.(mlab)*" (μg L"^-1*")")) +
    theme(legend.position='none',
          axis.text.x=element_blank()) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma(),limits = c(numLoc$y_num %>% min(),NA))
  
  if(met %in% c("K_Filtered","K_Total","Ca_Filtered","Ca_Total",
                "Mg_Filtered","Mg_Total","Na_Filtered","Na_Total")){
    ylab = paste0(sub(".*_","",met)," ",sub("_.*","",met))
    pt = pt +
      labs(y=bquote(.(ylab)*" (mg L"^-1*")"))
  } else if(met %in% names_met){
    ylab = paste0(sub(".*_","",met)," ",sub("_.*","",met))
    pt = pt +
      labs(y=bquote(.(ylab)*" (μg L"^-1*")"))
  } else if(met == "Ammonia"){
    pt = pt +
      labs(y=bquote("Ammonium (μg L"^-1*")"))
  } else if(met %in% mgs){
    pt = pt +
      labs(y=bquote(.(met)*" (mg L"^-1*")"))
  } else if(met %in% ugs){
    pt = pt +
      labs(y=bquote(.(met)*" (μg L"^-1*")"))
  } else if(met == "Temp"){
    pt = pt + 
      labs(y = "Temp (°C)")
  } else if(met == "Turbidity"){
    pt = pt + 
      labs(y = "Turbidity (NTU)")
  } else if(met == "Conductivity"){
    pt = pt + labs(y=bquote("Conductivity (μS cm"^-1*")"))
  } else if(met == "NPOC"){
    pt = pt + labs(y=bquote("DOC (mg L"^-1*")"))
  } else {
    pt = pt + 
      labs(y = met)
  }
  
  if(met == 'pH'){
    pt = pt + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(6,9.5))
  }
  
  pt
  
}

precip_plot = function(sampleDates = sampleDates,
                       bugDates = wide4,
                       precip = precip,
                       start='2020-01-01',end='2030-01-01',months = 2,
                       addBugs = F){
  
  start = '2022-01-01'
  end = '2022-12-02'
  
  samp_dates = sampleDates %>% pull(Date) %>% unique()

  samp_date_plt = tibble('Date' = samp_dates,
                         'Label' = 'Water Quality Sample')

  samp_date_plt = samp_date_plt %>%
    filter(Date >= start,
           Date <= end)
  
  bug_dates = bugDates %>%
    # Drops the outsourced run mid-July
    filter(!is.na(Species_Richness)) %>%
#    mutate(Date = as.Date(Date)) %>%
    pull(Date) %>% unique()
  
  bug_date_plt = tibble('Date' = bug_dates,
                        'Label' = 'Benthic Sample')
  bug_date_plt = bug_date_plt %>%
    filter(Date >= start,
           Date <= end)
  
  dataWQ2 = precip %>%
    filter(Date >= start,
           Date <= end) %>%
    mutate(Avg = Avg * 2.54)
  
  precip_point = ggplot() +
    geom_vline(data = samp_date_plt,
               aes(xintercept=Date,linetype = Label),
               linewidth=1.2,color='black') +
    geom_point(data = dataWQ2 %>%
                 filter(Avg>0),
               aes(x=Date,
                   y=Avg,
                   color=Type,shape=Type),
               size=6) +
  #  geom_errorbar(aes(x=Date,ymin=Min,ymax=Max),
#                width=2,size=1)+
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c(2))+
    labs(color='',shape='',linetype='',
         x = "Date (2022)",
         y='Water-Equivalent Precipitation (cm)') +
    bottomSlant

  if(months == 1){
    precip_point = precip_point + month_one
  } else if (months == 2){
    precip_point = precip_point + month_two
  } else {
    precip_point = precip_point + month_three
  }
  
  if(addBugs){
    precip_point = precip_point +
      geom_vline(data = bug_date_plt,
                 aes(xintercept = Date,linetype=Label),
                 linewidth=1.2,col='black') +
      scale_linetype_manual(values=c(1,3)) +
      scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b", date_breaks = '1 month')
  }
  
  precip_point
}

boxplot_bugs = function(dataIn=wide4,var){
  analytes = var
  # If the x-axis label angle isn't 90, then labels needs a horizontal offset 

  # Filter the input data to only include the analytes, Site and cbCol. cbCol 
  # is the colorblind friendly hex code associated with the sample site.
  # This tibble will be the tibble used in the final plot
  plt_data = dataIn %>%
    filter(!is.na(get(analytes))) %>%
    mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                          "Superior Associates",
                                          "Highway 36",
                                          "CTC Open Space"))) %>%
    select(Site,all_of(var),Colr) %>%
#    select(Site,all_of(analytes),Colr) %>%
    # Transform the data into a long tibble, where the multiple columns get 
    # transformed into Site, Analyte, Concentration, and cbCol
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(analytes),
                 names_to = "Analyte",
                 values_to = "Concentration") %>%
    # Filter to only include entries with a concentration that exist and > 0.
    #filter(!is.na(Concentration)) %>%
    #filter(Concentration > 0) %>%
    # Making the analyte column a factor keeps them in the order provided
    mutate(#Analyte = factor(Analyte,levels=analytes),
           # Create empty columns that values will be saved to later
           # for labels
           num = NA_real_,
           let = NA_character_,
           xpos = NA_real_,
           ypos_n = NA_real_,
           ypos_l = NA_real_)
  
 #plt_data$Site = plt_data$Site %>% droplevels()
  
  # Find the lowest concentration of all analytes, multiplied by 0.4 
  yn = plt_data %>% pull(Concentration) %>% min() * .4#- 
    #(plt_data %>% pull(Concentration) %>% max() *.1)
  
  if(analytes == "pH"){
    yn = 5
  } else if(analytes == "Total"){
    yn = -35
  }
  
  # Set the ypos_n to the previous value.
  # This will be the y-value for number of samples label
  plt_data$ypos_n = yn
  
  # A 'for loop' to get the wilcox values, calculate one analye at a time.
  for(i in 1:length(analytes)){
    # Save the analyte to be analyzed
    a = analytes[i]
    # Make a variable to track if there are enough values to
    # calculate a pValue
    pValue = T
    
    # Filter current data to only include rows with the current analyte.
    # Then group the rows by sample location, and calculate the number of 
    # samples at each site.
    dat = plt_data %>% filter(Analyte == a) %>%
      group_by(Site) %>%
      mutate(n = n())
    
    # Run a pairwise wilcox test on the concentrations between each site.
    # The output is a triangle matrix of p-values
    wilcox_result = pairwise.wilcox.test(dat %>% pull('Concentration'),
                                         dat %>% pull('Site'),
                                         p.adjust.method = 'none')
    
    # Check that there was enough data to run a wilcox test. If the pvalue 
    # matrix has 0 rows, then set 'pValue' to False so the labels get set 
    # correctly later.
    if(nrow(wilcox_result$p.value) == 0){
      pValue = F
    } else {
      # Use a  function to turn the triangle matrix into a square matrix 
      wilcox_matr = tri.to.squ(wilcox_result$p.value)
      
      # Use a function to turn the matrix of p-values into a list of letters 
      # for each site.Each site is given a letter based on the p-value 
      # relationships with each other site. If a p-value between sites
      # is less than or equal to 0.05, they will be marked as
      # different groups.
      let = multcompLetters(wilcox_matr,
                            compare="<=",
                            threshold = 0.05,
                            Letters = letters)
    }
    
    # Determines which sites have data for the current analyte. Another 
    # 'for loop' to  analyze one site at a time, and gather stats on that
    # site to make labels.
    for(j in 1:length(dat %>% pull(Site) %>% unique())){
        # save the site to look at. First way doesn't work right.
        #s = unique((dat$Site))[j]
        s = (dat$Site %>% levels)[j]
        # filter the single analyte data to only this single site. Save the 
        # value in column n, which is the number of samples at this site.
        n = dat %>% filter(Site == s) %>%  pull(n) %>% unique()
        
        # Save that n value as the number label in the plot column for 
        # this site and analyte pair
        plt_data$num[plt_data$Site == s & plt_data$Analyte == a] = n
        
        # Filter the single analyte data to only this site, and find the 
        # lowest concentration. save 90% of that value, to be used in 
        # next line
        yl = dat %>% filter(Site == s) %>% pull(Concentration) %>% min() * .8
        
        # Set that value as the y-axis location for the wilcox group label
        if(a == "Total"){
          plt_data$ypos_l[plt_data$Site == s & plt_data$Analyte == a] = yl - 10
        } else {
          plt_data$ypos_l[plt_data$Site == s & plt_data$Analyte == a] = yl
        }
        # Checks to make sure there are p-value groups for this site and 
        # analyte pair. If there are group letters, save the group letter 
        # for this site to be used next.
        if(pValue){
          l = let$Letters[[j]]
        } else {
          # If there's no p-value groups, use '--' instead of a letter
          l = '--'
        }
        # Save the letter value (or '--') to the plot tibble for the current
        # site analyte pair
        plt_data$let[plt_data$Site == s & plt_data$Analyte == a] = l
    }
  }
  
  # Save the list of hex code colors used for this plot
  #col_used = plt_data %>% pull(cbCol) %>% unique()
  
  # Find the used colors in the list of possible color codes. Matching in
  # this way ensures the color order matches the site order.
  #colrs = cbbPalette[cbbPalette %in% col_used]
  ylab = bugLab %>%
      filter(Name == var) %>%
      pull(Label)
    if(ylab %!in% c("Average HBTI","Invertebrate Count")){
      ylab = sub("Average HBTI","",ylab)
      ylab = sub("Count","",ylab)
      ylab = sub("Percent","",ylab)
    }
  cls = plt_data %>% arrange(Site) %>% select(Site,Colr) %>% unique() %>% pull(Colr)
  # Creates the plot from the tibble that has been populated
  plt = plt_data %>%
    ggplot() +
    # Sets the plot as a boxplot style, with the Analytes along the x-axis, 
    # concentration along y-axis, and the box fill color set by site.
    geom_boxplot(
      aes(x=Analyte,
          y=Concentration,
          fill=Site)) +
    # Use the saved color list as the colors used to fill the boxplot.
    scale_fill_manual(values = cls)+
    # Remove axis labels on the x axis and on the legend
    labs(x='',fill='') +
    bottomL +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow=2,byrow=T))

  # This next section is needed in case there is an inconsistent number 
  # of sites between analytes. This ensures the labels are placed correctly
  # along the x-axis. This step extracts graphical details about the plot.
  plt_info = ggplot_build(plt)$data[[1]]
  
  # Save a list of the x-positions of each box of the boxplot
  xpos = plt_info$x
  
  # xpos_n is used to keep track of which box x-labels being calculated for. 
  xpos_n = 1
  
  # These nested for loops check every analyte site pair, to find the x-axis
  # position of the corosponding box.
  for(i in 1:(length(analytes))){
    
    # Save the current analyte
    a = analytes[i]
    for(j in 1:length(plt_data$Site %>% unique())){
      # Save the current site to look at
      ste = levels(plt_data$Site)[j]
      
      # filter the plot data to the current site and analyte, and count the 
      # number of rows of data.
      nr = plt_data %>%filter(Site == ste, Analyte == a) %>% nrow()
      
      # If there are more than 0 rows, set the xpos column for the current 
      # analyte and site pair to the x-axis value the box is currently at.
      if(nr != 0){
        plt_data$xpos[plt_data$Analyte == a & plt_data$Site == ste] = xpos[xpos_n]
        
        # Add 1 to the variable keeping track of which box the x-axis
        # location has been extracted from
        xpos_n = xpos_n + 1
      }
    }
  }
  # After completing the above for loops, the x positions of labels have 
  # been saved to the plot data tibble.

  # Check if the logyes argument was set to True, or left to the 
  #default False value.
  
  # Updates to the plot adding the labels.
  plt = plt +
    # a text update to include number of samples at each analyte site pair.
    geom_text(data = plt_data,
              aes(x=xpos, y=ypos_n, label=num),
              # Use the label size value argument if provided, or use default
              size=labSize) +
    # Another text update to include the group letters across sites within 
    # an analyte.
    geom_text(data = plt_data,
              aes(x=xpos, y=ypos_l, label=let),
              size=labSize,
              # If a letAng value is provided, the string of letters will be 
              # rotated. adding a 45 degree rotation helps if sites have more 
              # than 3 group letters
              angle=letAng,
              hjust=hj) +
    # Adds the general plot formatting, of black axis lines
    # and no background
    bottomL +
    # Adds the y-label provided, or the default 'Concentration (µg/L)'
    labs(y=ylab) +
    # Set the text of the plot to the provided size, doesn't affect the 
    # geom_text labels.
    theme(text = element_text(size=txtSize),
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          legend.position = 'none')

  # Return the final plot as the output of the function
  plt
}

plot_list_box = function(dataIn=wide4,names){
  outList = vector(mode = 'list', length = length(names))
  for(n in names){
    plt = boxplot_bugs(var=n)
    outList[[which(n == names)]] = plt
  }
  outList
}

```

```{r read and tidy data}
sampleDates = read_csv("SampleDates.csv") %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date, format = "%d-%b-%Y"))

# Sometimes it's called regime_type
regime_type = sampleDates

wide4 = read_csv("Wide4.csv")

precip = read_csv("PrecipData.csv") %>%
  mutate(City = sub(" .*","",NAME),
         Date = as.Date(DATE,"%m/%d/%Y")) %>%
  filter(City %in% c("SUPERIOR","LOUISVILLE")) %>%
  select(STATION,City,Date,PRCP,SNOW,SNWD,WESF) %>%
  arrange(Date) %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  # Anytime there is snow, there is also precipitation
  # Conversion already done
  summarise(Avg_Snow = mean(SNOW,na.rm=T),
            Avg_Snow_Depth = mean(SNWD,na.rm=T),
            Avg_Snow_Water_Equiv = mean(WESF,na.rm=T),
            Avg_Precip = mean(PRCP,na.rm=T),
            Min_Precip = min(PRCP,na.rm=T),
            Max_Precip = max(PRCP,na.rm=T)) %>%
  replace(is.na(.),0) %>%
  rename(Avg = Avg_Precip,
         Min = Min_Precip,
         Max = Max_Precip) %>%
  select(Date,Avg)
  

bugData = read_csv("Bug_Data.csv") %>%
  group_by(Site,Date,Benthic) %>%
  # group benthics that were reclassified after data sheet was made
  mutate(Count = sum(Count,na.rm=T)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  unique() %>%
  mutate(FFG = case_when(
    FFG == "Detrivores" ~ "Detritivores",
    FFG == "Detritivore" ~ "Detritivores",
    T ~ FFG))

names_censors = names(wide4)[grepl("Cen",names(wide4))]
names_censors_filt = names_censors[grepl("Filtered",names_censors)]
names_filt = sub("Cen","",names_censors_filt)
names_censors_tots = names_censors[grepl("Total",names_censors)]
names_tots = sub("Cen","",names_censors_tots)
names_censors_noMet = setdiff(names_censors,
                              c(names_censors_tots,names_censors_filt))
names_noMet = sub("Cen","",names_censors_noMet)

names_met = c(names_filt, names_tots)

names_met_cen = c(names_censors_filt,names_censors_tots)
names_noMet_cen = names_censors_noMet

```

```{r sampling figure}
# Figure 3
sampFig = precip_plot(sampleDates, wide4, precip = precip, addBugs = T)
#sampFig
```

```{r bug plot 1}
names_bugs = names(wide4)[c(3:28,178,179)]

bugLab = tibble(
  Name = names_bugs,
  Label = sub("Avg","Average ",sub("_"," ",names_bugs))) %>%
  mutate(Label = case_when(
    Name == "Total" ~ "Invertebrate Count",
    Name == "Chl A" ~ "Chlorophyll-a",
    T ~ Label
  ))

# Values for the first bug boxplot
bug_general = c("Total","Shannon Diversity Index","Species_Richness",
                "Evenness","AvgHBTI")

labSize = 6
letAng = 0
hj = 0.5

bugbox1 = plot_grid(
  plotlist = plot_list_box(names = bug_general),
  align = 'vh')

# Figure 4
#bugbox1

# Make and save table of wilcox for bugs
bugAnalytes = names(wide4)[c(8,9,18,22,135,3,4,10,16,26,5,12,17,24,28)]

for(var in bugAnalytes){
  plt_data = wide4 %>%
    filter(!is.na(get(var))) %>%
    mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                          "Superior Associates",
                                          "Highway 36",
                                          "CTC Open Space"))) %>%
    select(Site,all_of(var)) %>%
    #    select(Site,all_of(analytes),Colr) %>%
    # Transform the data into a long tibble, where the multiple columns get 
    # transformed into Site, Analyte, Concentration, and cbCol
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(var),
                 names_to = "Analyte",
                 values_to = "Concentration") %>%
    # Filter to only include entries with a concentration that exist and > 0.
    #filter(!is.na(Concentration)) %>%
    #filter(Concentration > 0) %>%
    # Making the analyte column a factor keeps them in the order provided
    mutate(#Analyte = factor(Analyte,levels=analytes),
      # Create empty columns that values will be saved to later
      # for labels
      num = NA_real_,
      let = NA_character_,
      xpos = NA_real_,
      ypos_n = NA_real_,
      ypos_l = NA_real_)
  
  #for(i in 1:length(bugAnalytes)){
  # Save the analyte to be analyzed
  a = var#bugAnalytes[i]
  # Make a variable to track if there are enough values to
  # calculate a pValue
  pValue = T
  
  # Filter current data to only include rows with the current analyte.
  # Then group the rows by sample location, and calculate the number of 
  # samples at each site.
  dat = plt_data %>% filter(Analyte == a) %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    mutate(n = n())
  
  # Run a pairwise wilcox test on the concentrations between each site.
  # The output is a triangle matrix of p-values
  wilcox_result = pairwise.wilcox.test(dat %>% pull('Concentration'),
                                       dat %>% pull('Site'),
                                       p.adjust.method = 'none')
  
  # Check that there was enough data to run a wilcox test. If the pvalue 
  # matrix has 0 rows, then set 'pValue' to False so the labels get set 
  # correctly later.
  if(nrow(wilcox_result$p.value) == 0){
    pValue = F
  } else {
    # Use a  function to turn the triangle matrix into a square matrix 
    wilcox_matr = tri.to.squ(wilcox_result$p.value)
    
    # Use a function to turn the matrix of p-values into a list of letters 
    # for each site.Each site is given a letter based on the p-value 
    # relationships with each other site. If a p-value between sites
    # is less than or equal to 0.05, they will be marked as
    # different groups.
    let = multcompLetters(wilcox_matr,
                          compare="<=",
                          threshold = 0.05,
                          Letters = letters)
  }
  
  # Determines which sites have data for the current analyte. Another 
  # 'for loop' to  analyze one site at a time, and gather stats on that
  # site to make labels.
  for(j in 1:length(dat %>% pull(Site) %>% unique())){
    # save the site to look at. First way doesn't work right.
    #s = unique((dat$Site))[j]
    s = (dat$Site %>% levels)[j]
    # filter the single analyte data to only this single site. Save the 
    # value in column n, which is the number of samples at this site.
    n = dat %>% filter(Site == s) %>%  pull(n) %>% unique()
    
    # Save that n value as the number label in the plot column for 
    # this site and analyte pair
    plt_data$num[plt_data$Site == s & plt_data$Analyte == a] = n
    
    # Filter the single analyte data to only this site, and find the 
    # lowest concentration. save 90% of that value, to be used in 
    # next line
    yl = dat %>% filter(Site == s) %>% pull(Concentration) %>% min() * .8
    
    # Set that value as the y-axis location for the wilcox group label
    if(a == "Total"){
      plt_data$ypos_l[plt_data$Site == s & plt_data$Analyte == a] = yl - 10
    } else {
      plt_data$ypos_l[plt_data$Site == s & plt_data$Analyte == a] = yl
    }
    # Checks to make sure there are p-value groups for this site and 
    # analyte pair. If there are group letters, save the group letter 
    # for this site to be used next.
    if(pValue){
      l = let$Letters[[j]]
    } else {
      # If there's no p-value groups, use '--' instead of a letter
      l = '--'
    }
    # Save the letter value (or '--') to the plot tibble for the current
    # site analyte pair
    plt_data$let[plt_data$Site == s & plt_data$Analyte == a] = l
  }
  
  bug_wilc_new = plt_data %>%
    select(Analyte,Site,let,num) %>%
    unique()
  
  if(which(var == bugAnalytes) == 1){
    bugWilc = bug_wilc_new
  } else{
    bugWilc = bugWilc %>%
      add_row(bug_wilc_new)
  }
}

bugWilcWide = bugWilc %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Site,
              values_from = c("let","num"))

# Table S9 is bugWilcWide with some names cleaned up
#bugWilcWide

```

```{r chla plots}
ffgData = wide4 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Total)) %>%
  select(Date,Site,contains("_Count")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(Date,Site),
              names_to = "FFG",
              values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(FFG = sub("_Count","",FFG),
         Site = factor(Site,levels = bugSites)) %>%
  filter(Date != "2022-07-12")

bugDates = ffgData %>%
  filter(Site == "CTC Open Space") %>%
  select(Date) %>%
  unique() %>% 
  pull(Date)


combos = expand.grid(Date = bugDates,
                     Site = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                        "Superior Associates",
                                        "Highway 36",
                                        "CTC Open Space"),
                     FFG = ffgData %>% pull(FFG) %>% unique()) %>% as_tibble()

# Add NAs when site was dry
for(i in 1:nrow(combos)){
  rowAdd = combos %>%
    slice(i) %>%
    mutate(Count = NA_real_)
  inBugs = ffgData %>%
    filter(Date == rowAdd$Date,
           Site == rowAdd$Site,
           FFG == rowAdd$FFG)
  if(nrow(inBugs) == 0){
    ffgData = ffgData %>%
      bind_rows(rowAdd)
  }
}

colsLine = palette.colors(palette = "R4", n = 7)
# Updated to include gaps
plot = ffgData %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Date, y=Count)) +
  geom_line(aes(color = FFG),
            linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(.~Site) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(x="",color='',
       y = "Number of Invertebrates") +
  scale_color_manual(values = colsLine)+
  scale_x_date(date_breaks  = 'months', date_labels = "%b") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'white')
  ) +
  bottomSlant +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1,vjust=1),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
        panel.spacing = unit(1,'lines'))
#  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 18))

#plot
# Figure 5a
# Save as 1200 x 800

chla_data = wide4 %>%
  select(Date,Site,Chl_A) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Chl_A)) %>%
  mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = bugSites))

plotC = ggplot(chla_data) +
  geom_line(aes(x=Date,y=Chl_A,color=Site),
            linewidth = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cbLookup[bugSites]) +
  bottomSlant +
  labs(y=bquote("Chlorophyll-a (mg m"^-2*')'), x = "", color = '') +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = 'month' , date_labels = "%b")
  
#plotC

# Saved as 1200 x 1000 for Figure 5b
```

```{r bug table at sites}
ffgOrd = c("Scrapers","Gathering Collectors",
           "Filtering Collectors", "Shredders",
           "Predators/Detritivores", "Multiple")

numSiteSamp = bugData %>%
  select(Site,Date) %>%
  unique() %>%
  group_by(Site) %>%
  summarise(n=n()) %>%
  mutate(bold = ceiling(n*0.8))

bugTable = bugData %>%
  select(Site,Benthic,Count,HBI) %>%
  group_by(Site,Benthic) %>%
  mutate(Count = sum(Count,na.rm=T),
         n = n()) %>%
  unique() %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    Abundance = case_when(
      Count > 50 ~ "XXX",
      Count >= 11 ~ "XX",
      Count >= 1 ~ "X",
      T ~ "-"),
    Bold = case_when(
      Site == "CTC Open Space" & n >= 8 ~ T,
      Site == "Greenbelt Plateau" & n >= 5 ~ T,
      Site == "Highway 36" & n >= 8 ~ T,
      Site == "Superior Associates" & n >= 5 ~ T,
      T ~ F),
    Abundance = case_when(
      Bold ~ paste0(Abundance,"-Bold"),
      T ~ Abundance
    )) %>%
  left_join(bugData %>% 
              select(Benthic,FFG) %>%
              unique()) %>%
  select(FFG,Benthic,HBI,Site,Abundance) %>%
  mutate(FFG = case_when(
    # Grouping for this table for readability
    Benthic == "Tricladida" ~ "Predators/Detritivores",
    FFG %in% c("Predators","Detritivores") ~ "Predators/Detritivores",
    FFG == "-" ~ "Multiple",
    T ~ FFG)) %>%
  mutate(FFG = factor(FFG, levels = ffgOrd),
         Site = factor(Site, levels = bugSites)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Site, values_from = Abundance) %>%
  arrange(FFG,Benthic) %>%
  mutate(`Greenbelt Plateau` = replace_na(`Greenbelt Plateau`,"-"),
         `Highway 36` = replace_na(`Highway 36`,"-"),
         `CTC Open Space` = replace_na(`CTC Open Space`,"-"),
         `Superior Associates` = replace_na(`Superior Associates`,"-")) %>%
  select(FFG,Benthic,HBI,all_of(bugSites))


# Table 2
# bugTable
```

```{r Scrapers per chlA}
pdat = wide4 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Scrapers_Count) & !is.na(Chl_A)) %>%
  rename(y=Scrapers_Count,x=Chl_A)

 plt_chla_scrap = ggplot(data = wide4 %>%
                           filter(!is.na(Scrapers_Count) & !is.na(Chl_A)),
                         aes(x=Chl_A, y=Scrapers_Count)) +
   geom_point(aes(shape = Site, color=Site),
              size = 6) +
   scale_color_manual(values = cbLookupBug) +
   scale_shape_manual(values = shapesBug) +
   bottomL +
   labs(x = bquote("Chlorophyll A (mg/m"^2*")"),
        y = "Number of Scrapers",
        shape = "", color = "") +
   guides(color=guide_legend(nrow=2,byrow=F))+
   geom_smooth(method = 'lm',aes(color=Site),
               se=F)

 tS = 33
 pS = 7
 
 slope_CIs = tibble(
   Site = character(),
   SlopeMin = numeric(),
   SlopeMax = numeric()
 )
 
chl_GBP = pdat %>% 
  filter(Site == "Greenbelt Plateau") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=x,y=y))+
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm',aes(color=Site)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape=Site,color=Site), size = pS) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cbLookupBug[1]) +
  #scale_shape_manual(values = shapesBug[1]) +
  bottomL +
  labs(x = bquote("Chlorophyll-a (mg m"^-2*")"),
       y = "Number of Scrapers",
       shape = "", color = "") +
  guides(color=guide_legend(nrow=2,byrow=F))+
  
  geom_text(x = 2, y = 80, label = lm_eqn(pdat %>% 
                                            filter(Site == "Greenbelt Plateau")),
            parse = TRUE,
            size = 10) +
  geom_text(x = 2, y = 70, label = lm_eqn2(pdat %>% 
                                            filter(Site == "Greenbelt Plateau")),
            parse = TRUE,
            size = 10) +
  coord_cartesian(clip='off', ylim=c(-10,110),xlim=c(0,7)) +
  theme(text = element_text(size=tS),
        axis.text = element_text(size=tS),
        legend.position = 'inside',
        legend.position.inside=c(0.25,0.9))
  # scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,7)) +
  # scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,110))

sCI = confint(lm(y ~ x, data = pdat %>% filter(Site == "Greenbelt Plateau")))[2,]

slope_CIs = slope_CIs %>%
  bind_rows(
    tibble(
      Site = "GBP",
      SlopeMin = as.numeric(sCI[1]),
      SlopeMax = as.numeric(sCI[2])
    ))

chl_SA = pdat %>% 
  filter(Site == "Superior Associates") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=x,y=y))+
  geom_point(aes(shape=Site,color=Site), size = pS) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cbLookupBug[2]) +
  #scale_shape_manual(values = shapesBug[2]) +
  bottomL +
  labs(x = bquote("Chlorophyll-a (mg m"^-2*")"),
       y = "Number of Scrapers",
       shape = "", color = "") +
  guides(color=guide_legend(nrow=2,byrow=F))+
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm',aes(color=Site), se=T,fullrange = T) +
  geom_text(x = 2, y = 40, label = lm_eqn(pdat %>% 
                                            filter(Site == "Superior Associates")),
            parse = TRUE,
            size = 10)+
  geom_text(x = 2, y = 30, label = lm_eqn2(pdat %>% 
                                            filter(Site == "Superior Associates")),
            parse = TRUE,
            size = 10)+
#  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,7)) +
#  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-10,110))
  coord_cartesian(clip='off', ylim=c(-10,110),xlim=c(0,7))+
  theme(text = element_text(size=tS),
        axis.text = element_text(size=tS),
        legend.position = 'inside',
        legend.position.inside=c(0.25,0.9))

sCI = confint(lm(y ~ x, data = pdat %>% filter(Site == "Superior Associates")))[2,]

slope_CIs = slope_CIs %>%
  bind_rows(
    tibble(
      Site = "SA",
      SlopeMin = as.numeric(sCI[1]),
      SlopeMax = as.numeric(sCI[2])
    ))

chl_H36 = pdat %>% 
  filter(Site == "Highway 36") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=x,y=y))+
  geom_point(aes(shape=Site,color=Site), size = pS) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cbLookupBug[3]) +
  #scale_shape_manual(values = shapesBug[3]) +
  bottomL +
  labs(x = bquote("Chlorophyll-a (mg m"^-2*")"),
       y = "Number of Scrapers",
       shape = "", color = "") +
  guides(color=guide_legend(nrow=2,byrow=F))+
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm',aes(color=Site)) +
  geom_text(x = 1.5, y = 30, label = lm_eqn(pdat %>% 
                                            filter(Site == "Highway 36")),
            parse = TRUE,
            size = 10)+
  geom_text(x = 1.5, y = 20, label = lm_eqn2(pdat %>% 
                                            filter(Site == "Highway 36")),
            parse = TRUE,
            size = 10)+
  coord_cartesian(clip='off', ylim=c(-10,110),xlim=c(0,7))+
  theme(text = element_text(size=tS),
        axis.text = element_text(size=tS),
        legend.position = 'inside',
        legend.position.inside=c(0.25,0.9))
  # scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,7)) +
  # scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,110))

sCI = confint(lm(y ~ x, data = pdat %>% filter(Site == "Highway 36")))[2,]

slope_CIs = slope_CIs %>%
  bind_rows(
    tibble(
      Site = "Hwy36",
      SlopeMin = as.numeric(sCI[1]),
      SlopeMax = as.numeric(sCI[2])
    ))

chl_CTC = pdat %>% 
  filter(Site == "CTC Open Space") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=x,y=y))+
  geom_point(aes(shape=Site,color=Site), size = pS) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cbLookupBug[4]) +
  #scale_shape_manual(values = shapesBug[4]) +
  bottomL +
  labs(x = bquote("Chlorophyll-a (mg m"^-2*")"),
       y = "Number of Scrapers",
       shape = "", color = "") +
  guides(color=guide_legend(nrow=2,byrow=F))+
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm',aes(color=Site)) +
  geom_text(x = 3, y = 80, label = lm_eqn(pdat %>% 
                                            filter(Site == "CTC Open Space")),
            parse = TRUE,
            size = 10)+
  geom_text(x = 3, y = 70, label = lm_eqn2(pdat %>% 
                                            filter(Site == "CTC Open Space")),
            parse = TRUE,
            size = 10)+
  coord_cartesian(clip='off', ylim=c(-10,110),xlim=c(0,7))+
  theme(text = element_text(size=tS),
        axis.text = element_text(size=tS),
        legend.position = 'inside',
        legend.position.inside=c(0.25,0.9))
  # scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,7)) +
  # scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,110))


sCI = confint(lm(y ~ x, data = pdat %>% filter(Site == "CTC Open Space")))[2,]

slope_CIs = slope_CIs %>%
  bind_rows(
    tibble(
      Site = "CTC",
      SlopeMin = as.numeric(sCI[1]),
      SlopeMax = as.numeric(sCI[2])
    ))
  
chlA_ScrapPlts = plot_grid(plotlist = list(chl_GBP,chl_SA,chl_H36,chl_CTC))

#slope_CIs
# Figure 6
#chlA_ScrapPlts
```

```{r Calculate exceedances plots and table}
# Caculate exceedances for both CO and EPA,
# Option to save off data to read in other places
# Generate Table S7, and Figure 7
totCs = paste0(c("Al"),"_Total")
filtCs = paste0(c("Cr","Cu","Pb","Mn","Ni","U","Zn"),"_Filtered")

excd = wide4 %>%
  filter(Date < '2023-01-01') %>%
  arrange(Date) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Hardness)) %>%
  select(Date,Site,Hardness,all_of(c(totCs,filtCs))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(1:3),names_to='Analyte',values_to = "Concentration") %>%
  mutate(acuteLim = NA_real_,
         acuteExcd = F,
         chronLim = NA_real_,
         chronExcd = F)

for(i in 1:nrow(excd)){
  # using Colorado exceedance calculations
  #tv = excd$TotalVsFiltered[i]
  m = excd$Analyte[i]
  h = excd$Hardness[i]
  if(h > 400){
    h = 400
  }
  if(grepl("Al",m) & h > 220){
    h = 220
  }
    cLim = case_when(
      # Filtered Chronic
      m == "Cr_Filtered" ~ exp(0.819*(log(h))+0.534),
      m == "Cu_Filtered" ~ exp(0.8545*(log(h))-1.7428),
      m == "Pb_Filtered" ~ (1.46203-((log(h))*0.145712))*(exp(1.273*(log(h))-4.705)),
      m == "Mn_Filtered" ~ exp(0.3331*(log(h))+5.8743),
      m == "Ni_Filtered" ~ exp(0.846*(log(h))+0.0554),
      m == "U_Filtered" ~ exp(1.1021*(log(h))+2.2382),
      m == "Zn_Filtered" ~ 0.986*exp(0.9094*(log(h))+0.6235),
      m == "Al_Total" ~ exp(1.3695*(log(h))-0.1158)
      )
    aLim = case_when(
      # Filtered Acute
      m == "Cr_Filtered" ~ exp(0.819*(log(h))+2.5736),
      m == "Cu_Filtered" ~ exp(0.9422*(log(h))-1.7408),
      m == "Pb_Filtered" ~ (1.46203-((log(h))*(0.145712)))*(exp(1.273*(log(h))-1.46)),
      m == "Mn_Filtered" ~ exp(0.3331*(log(h))+6.4676),
      m == "Ni_Filtered" ~ exp(0.846*(log(h))+2.253),
      m == "U_Filtered" ~ exp(1.1021*(log(h))+2.7088),
      m == "Zn_Filtered" ~ 0.978*exp(0.9094*(log(h))+0.9095),
      m == "Al_Total" ~ exp(1.3695*(log(h))+1.8308)

    )
  excd$acuteLim[i] = aLim
  excd$chronLim[i] = cLim
}

excd = excd %>%
  mutate(acuteExcd = Concentration >= acuteLim,
         chronExcd = Concentration >= chronLim)

epaMet = c("Cu","Ni","Pb","Zn","Cd")
epaCs = c(paste0(epaMet,"_Filtered"),
          paste0(epaMet,"_Total"))

excdEPA = wide4 %>%
  #filter(!is.na(SampleID)) %>%
  filter(Date < '2023-01-01') %>%
  #group_by(SampleID) %>%
  # mutate(Hardness = case_when(
  #   TotalVsFiltered == "Filtered" ~ (Ca/1000*2.497 + Mg/1000*4.118),
  #   T ~ NA_real_)) %>%
  # mutate(Hardness = case_when(
  #   TotalVsFiltered == "Total" ~ lag(Hardness),
  #   T ~ Hardness
  # )) %>%
  arrange(Date) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(Date,Site,Hardness,all_of(epaCs)) %>%
#  select(Date,Site,TotalVsFiltered,Hardness,Cu,Ni,Pb,Zn) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(1:3),names_to='Analyte',values_to = "Concentration") %>%
  mutate(acuteLim = NA_real_,
         acuteExcd = F,
         chronLim = NA_real_,
         chronExcd = F)

for(i in 1:nrow(excdEPA)){
  #tv = excdEPA$TotalVsFiltered[i]
  m = excdEPA$Analyte[i]
  h = excdEPA$Hardness[i]
#  if(tv == "Filtered"){
    cLim = case_when(
      m == "Cu_Filtered" ~ (exp(0.8545*log(h)-1.702))*(0.96),
      m == "Ni_Filtered" ~ (exp(0.846*log(h)+0.0584))*(0.997),
      m == "Pb_Filtered" ~ (exp(1.273*log(h)-4.705))*(1.46203-(log(h)*0.145712)),
      m == "Zn_Filtered" ~ (exp(0.8473*log(h)+0.884))*(0.986),
      m == "Cd_Filtered" ~ (exp(0.7977*log(h)-3.909))*(1.101672-(log(h)*0.041838)),
#      m == "Cu_Total" ~ (exp(0.8545*log(h)-1.702)),
#      m == "Ni_Total" ~ (exp(0.846*log(h)+0.0584)),
      m == "Ni_Total" ~ 52,
#      m == "Pb_Total" ~ (exp(1.273*log(h)-4.705)),
      m == "Pb_Total" ~ 2.5,
#      m == "Zn_Total" ~ (exp(0.8473*log(h)+0.884)),
      m == "Zn_Total" ~ 120
#      m == "Cd_Total" ~ (exp(0.7977*log(h)-3.909))
    )
    aLim = case_when(
      m == "Cu_Filtered" ~ (exp(0.9422*log(h)-1.7))*(0.96),
      m == "Ni_Filtered" ~ (exp(0.846*log(h)+2.255))*(0.998),
      m == "Pb_Filtered" ~ (exp(1.273*log(h)-1.46))*(1.46203-(log(h)*0.145712)),
      m == "Zn_Filtered" ~ (exp(0.8473*log(h)+0.884))*(0.978),
      m == "Cd_Filtered" ~ (exp(0.9789*log(h)-3.866))*(1.136672-(log(h)*0.041838)),
#      m == "Cu_Total" ~ (exp(0.9422*log(h)-1.7)),
#      m == "Ni_Total" ~ (exp(0.846*log(h)+2.255)),
      m == "Ni_Total" ~ 470,
#      m == "Pb_Total" ~ (exp(1.273*log(h)-1.46)),
      m == "Pb_Total" ~ 65,
#      m == "Zn_Total" ~ (exp(0.8473*log(h)+0.884)),
      m == "Zn_Total" ~ 120,
      m == "Cd_Total" ~ 1.8
#      m == "Cd_Total" ~ (exp(0.9789*log(h)-3.866))
    )
#  } else {
    # cLim = case_when(
    # 
    # )
    # aLim = case_when(
    #   
    # )
#  }
  excdEPA$acuteLim[i] = aLim
  excdEPA$chronLim[i] = cLim
}

# exceedancesEPA = excdEPA  %>%
#   mutate(acuteExcd = Concentration >= acuteLim,
#          chronExcd = Concentration >= chronLim) %>%
#   filter(chronExcd)

excdEPA = excdEPA %>%
  mutate(acuteExcd = Concentration >= acuteLim,
         chronExcd = Concentration >= chronLim) %>%
  rename(acuteLimEPA = acuteLim,
         acuteExcdEPA = acuteExcd,
         chronLimEPA = chronLim,
         chronExcdEPA = chronExcd)


allExceedances = full_join(excdEPA,
                           excd)

##write_csv(allExceedances,"AllExceedanceInfo.csv")

excd_output = allExceedances %>%
  filter(chronExcd | chronExcdEPA) %>%
  select(Date,Site,Hardness,Analyte,Concentration,
         chronLim,chronLimEPA,
         acuteLim,acuteLimEPA,
         chronExcd,chronExcdEPA,
         acuteExcd,acuteExcdEPA) %>%
  rename("CO Chronic Limit" = chronLim,
         "CO Acute Limit" = acuteLim,
         "EPA Chronic Limit" = chronLimEPA,
         "EPA Acute Limit" = acuteLimEPA) %>%
  mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels =  c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                          "Superior Associates",
                                          "Highway 36",
                                          "CTC Open Space")))
  #filter(Site != "Greenbelt Plateau") %>%
  #filter(Site != "Mulberry")
  
##write_csv(excd_output,"Ch3_Exceedances_CO_EPA.csv")

##excd_yrIn = read_csv("Ch3_Exceedances_CO_EPA.csv")
excd_yrIn = excd_output

excd_yr = excd_yrIn %>%
  select(-c(chronExcd,chronExcdEPA,acuteExcd,acuteExcdEPA)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(Date,Site,Hardness,Analyte,Concentration),
               names_to = "LimitType",
               values_to = "LimitVal") %>%
  filter(!is.na(LimitVal)) %>%
  mutate(PercentLimit = round(100*Concentration/LimitVal,2),
         Date = as.Date(Date,format = "%m/%d/%Y")) %>%
  filter(PercentLimit >= 100) %>%
  left_join(regime_type, by="Date") %>%
  rename(FlowRegime = Type) %>%
  select(FlowRegime,Date,Site,Analyte,Concentration,LimitType,PercentLimit,LimitVal)

##write_csv(excd_yr, "OneYear_Exceedance.csv")

excd_yrUse = excd_yr %>%
#excd_yrUse = read_csv("OneYear_Exceedance.csv") %>%
  mutate(Excd = T) %>%
  select(Date,Site,Analyte,Excd)

excd_mets = excd_yrUse$Analyte %>% unique()
excd_metsC = sub("_","Cen_",excd_mets)

sites = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
          "Superior Associates",
          "Highway 36",
          "CTC Open Space")

siteEx = paste0(sites," Exceedance")

long4Vals = wide4 %>%
  select(Date,Site,all_of(excd_mets),Shp,Colr,Type,Hardness) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(Date,Site,Shp,Colr,Type, Hardness),
               names_to = "Analyte",
               values_to = "Concentration")

long4Cens = wide4 %>%
  select(Date,Site,all_of(excd_metsC)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(Date,Site),
               names_to = "Analyte",
               values_to = "Censored") %>%
  mutate(Analyte = sub("Cen","",Analyte),
         Censored = case_when(Censored == "0" ~ F,
                              T ~ T))

# Table S7:
tableS7 = left_join(excd_yr, long4Cens) %>%
  filter(!Censored) %>%
  mutate(tvf = sub(".*_","",Analyte)) %>%
  arrange(Date,tvf,Analyte,Site) %>%
  select(FlowRegime,Date,Site,LimitType,Analyte,Concentration,LimitVal,PercentLimit)

#write_csv(tableS7,"TableS7.csv")

long4ExAll = long4Vals %>%
  left_join(long4Cens, by = c("Date","Site","Analyte")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Concentration)) %>%
  left_join(excd_yrUse,by=c("Date","Site","Analyte")) %>%
  mutate(Excd = case_when(is.na(Excd) ~ F,
                          T ~ T),
         Shp = case_when(
           Shp == 17 ~ 24,
           Shp == 15 ~ 22,
           Shp == 18 ~ 23,
           Shp == 16 ~ 21 )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Concentration)) %>%
  mutate(ExcdName = case_when(Excd ~ paste0(Site," Exceedance"),
                          T ~ Site),
         ExcdName = factor(ExcdName, levels = c(sites, siteEx)),
         Site = factor(Site, levels = sites)) %>%
  unique()

long4Ex = long4ExAll %>%
  filter(Analyte %!in% c("Cd_Total","Cd_Filtered","Cr_Filtered")) %>%
  mutate(Analyte = paste0(
    sub("_"," ",Analyte)),
    Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Snowmelt","Baseflow","Rain")),
    FilCol = case_when(
      Excd ~ "#FFFFFF",
      T ~ Colr))

shps = long4Ex %>% arrange(ExcdName) %>% pull(Shp) %>% unique()
shps2a = rep(shps,2)
colrs = long4Ex %>% arrange(ExcdName) %>% pull(Colr) %>% unique()
fils2a = c(colrs,rep("#FFFFFF",4))
colrs2a = rep(colrs,2)


# pltLeg = ggplot() +
#   geom_point(
#     data = long4Ex,
#     aes(x=Date,y=Concentration,
#         color = ExcdName,
#         fill = ExcdName,
#         shape = ExcdName),
#     size = 6, stroke = 1.5) +
#   scale_shape_manual(values = shps2a) +
#       scale_color_manual(values = colrs2a) +
#       scale_fill_manual(values = fils2a) +
#   bottomL +
#   labs(fill = '', shape = '', color = '')

long4ExVal = long4Ex %>% filter(!Censored)
long4ExCen = long4Ex %>%
  filter(Censored) %>%
  mutate(DateDif = case_when(
      Site == "Greenbelt Plateau" ~ 0.6,
      Site == "Superior Associates" ~ 0.2,
      Site == "Highway 36" ~ 0.4,
      Site == "Dutch Creek Park" ~ 0,
      Site == "CTC Open Space" ~ 0.8,
      Site == "Mulberry" ~ 0),
      barW = case_when(
        Type == "Baseflow" ~ 3,
        Type == "Rain" ~ 2,
        Type == "Snowmelt" ~ 1))

regs = c("Snowmelt","Rain","Baseflow")
exMetsA = long4ExVal %>% 
  group_by(Analyte) %>%
  filter(Excd) %>%
  mutate(n=n()) %>%
  arrange(-n) %>%
  pull(Analyte) %>%
  unique()

exMetsT = exMetsA[c(1,3,4,6)]
exMetsF = exMetsA[c(2,5,7,8)]

exMets = exMetsF
exMets = c("Zn Filtered", "Cu Filtered","Ni Filtered", "Pb Filtered")

exPlts = vector(mode = 'list', length = length(exMets)*length(regs))
i = 0
pltScale = tibble(
  Met = exMets,
  Max = rep(0,length(exMets))
)
for(em in exMets){
  em2 = sub(" .*","",em)
  em1 = sub(".* ","",em)
  for(r in regs){
      i = i + 1
    datV = long4ExVal %>%
      filter(Type == r, Analyte == em)
    
    datC = long4ExCen %>%
      filter(Type == r, Analyte == em)
    
    shps2 = datV %>% arrange(ExcdName) %>% select(ExcdName, Shp) %>% unique() %>% pull(Shp)
    colrs2 = datV %>% arrange(ExcdName) %>% select(ExcdName, Colr) %>% unique() %>% pull(Colr)
    fils2 = datV %>% arrange(ExcdName) %>% select(ExcdName, FilCol) %>% unique() %>% pull(FilCol)
    
    p = ggplot()+
      geom_point(data = datV,
                 aes(x=Date, y=Concentration,
                     shape = ExcdName, color = ExcdName, fill=ExcdName),
                 size = 4, stroke = 1.5) +
      scale_shape_manual(values = shps2) +
      scale_color_manual(values = colrs2) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = fils2) +
      geom_segment(data = datC,
               color = datC$Colr,
               aes(x=Date+DateDif, xend=Date+DateDif,
                   y=0,yend=Concentration),
                   linewidth = 1.5, linetype = 1,
                   alpha = .5) +
      bottomL +
  geom_segment(data = datC,
               color = datC$Colr,
               aes(x=Date-barW+DateDif, xend=Date+barW+DateDif,
                   y=Concentration,yend=Concentration),
                 linewidth = 1.5, linetype = 1,
                 alpha = .5) +
      scale_x_date(date_labels = dateFormat,
                 date_breaks = '1 month',
                 limits = as.Date(c("2022-01-01","2022-11-29"))) + 
    labs(shape = "", color = "", linetype = "", x = "", y = "") +
    theme(legend.position = 'none',
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          text = element_text(size=txtSize)) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma())
    
    if(r == "Snowmelt"){
      p = p +
        labs(y = bquote(.(em1)*" "*.(em2)*" (μg L"^-1*")"))
    }
    if(em == exMets[length(exMets)]){
      p = p +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1,vjust=1))
    }
    if(em == exMets[1]){
      p = p + labs(title = r) + theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
    }
    
    maxY = layer_scales(p)$y$range$range[2]
    pltScale = pltScale %>%
      mutate(Max = case_when(
        Met == em & Max < maxY ~ maxY,
        T ~ Max
      ))
    exPlts[[i]] = p
  }
}

for(i in 1:length(exPlts)){
  mv = exMets[ceiling(i/3)]
  print(mv)
  maxYr = pltScale %>%
    filter(Met == mv) %>%
    pull(Max)
  
  exPlts[[i]] = exPlts[[i]] +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(NA,maxYr))
  
}

exPlt = plot_grid(plotlist = exPlts, ncol = 3, align = 'v')

# ggsave("Figures/Figure7.jpg",
#        exPlt,
#        device = 'jpg',
#        width = 23,
#        height = 20)

```

```{r generalized wilcox}
# Functions used to calculated generalized wilcox value
#  The output is saved to ./GeneralWilcox_All so this chunk
#  doesn't need to be run again, but it can be without issue since the save
#  is commented out
lineToTri = function(lineDat){
  cnames = lineDat[,1] %>% unique() %>% pull()
  rnames = lineDat[,2] %>% unique() %>% pull()
  nc = rnames %>% length()
  nr = cnames %>% length()
  outMatrix = matrix(nrow = nr, ncol = nc)
  rownames(outMatrix) = rnames
  colnames(outMatrix) = cnames
  lineInd = c(1:nrow(lineDat))
  matrInd = c()
  for(i in 1:nc){
    stp = i*nc
    strt = i*nc - (nc-i)
    matrInd = c(matrInd,(strt:stp))
  }
  for(i in 1:length(lineInd)){
    outMatrix[matrInd[i]] = lineDat$PValue[i]
  }
  outMatrix
}

pairwise_cendiff_1yr = function(dataIn,pVals = F){
  out_tib = tibble(Site1 = character(),
                   Site2 = character(),
                   PValue = numeric())
  
  metal = names(dataIn)[3]
  metalCen = names(dataIn)[4]
  
  sites = dataIn$Site %>% unique()
  
  for(i in 1:(length(sites)-1)){
    for(j in (i+1):length(sites)){
      s1 = sites[i]
      s2 = sites[j]
      dataComp = dataIn %>%
        filter(Site %in% c(s1,s2)) %>%
        mutate(Cen = .[[4]] == "0")
      
      genWilcR = survdiff(Surv(get(metal),Cen) ~ Site,data=dataComp)#,
               #TotalVsFiltered == tvd) %>%
        #select(Site, all_of(c(metal,metalCen)))
      #if(sum(grepl("G",dataComp[,3])) > 0){next}
      #obs = dataComp %>% pull(metal)
      #cenLet = dataComp %>% pull(metalCen)
      #censored = cenLet != "0"
      #groups = dataComp %>% pull(Site) %>% factor()
      #genWilc = cendiff(obs,censored,groups)
      newRow = tibble(
        "Site1" = s1, "Site2" = s2, "PValue" = genWilcR$pvalue)
      out_tib = add_row(out_tib, newRow)
    }
  }
  if(pVals){
    out_tib
  } else {
    tri_mat = lineToTri(out_tib)
    squ_mat = tri.to.squ(tri_mat)
    output = multcompLetters(squ_mat,
                             compare="<=",
                             threshold = 0.05,
                             Letters = letters)
    out_tib2 = tibble(
      "Site" = output$Letters %>% names(),
      "Vals" = output$Letters %>% as.character()) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Site, values_from = Vals)
    
    out_tib2
  }
}

# Needs different logic for right censored
pairwise_cendiff_1yr_right = function(dataIn,pVals = F){
  out_tib = tibble(Site1 = character(),
                   Site2 = character(),
                   PValue = numeric())
  
  metal = names(dataIn)[3]
  metalCen = names(dataIn)[4]
  
  sites = dataIn$Site %>% unique()
  
  for(i in 1:(length(sites)-1)){
    for(j in (i+1):length(sites)){
      s1 = sites[i]
      s2 = sites[j]
      dataComp = dataIn %>%
        filter(Site %in% c(s1,s2))#,
               #TotalVsFiltered == tvd) %>%
        #select(Site, all_of(c(metal,metalCen)))
      #if(sum(grepl("G",dataComp[,3])) > 0){next}
      obs = dataComp %>% pull(metal)
      cenLet = dataComp %>% pull(metalCen)
      censored = cenLet != "0"
      groups = dataComp %>% pull(Site) %>% factor()
      genWilc = cendiff(obs,censored,groups)
      newRow = tibble(
        "Site1" = s1, "Site2" = s2, "PValue" = genWilc$pvalue)
      out_tib = add_row(out_tib, newRow)
    }
  }
  if(pVals){
    out_tib
  } else {
    tri_mat = lineToTri(out_tib)
    squ_mat = tri.to.squ(tri_mat)
    output = multcompLetters(squ_mat,
                             compare="<=",
                             threshold = 0.05,
                             Letters = letters)
    out_tib2 = tibble(
      "Site" = output$Letters %>% names(),
      "Vals" = output$Letters %>% as.character()) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Site, values_from = Vals)
    
    out_tib2
  }
}
names_censors = names(wide4)[grepl("Cen",names(wide4))]

# Save by flow regime (reg) or overall
genWilc_p_val_reg = tibble(
  Analyte = character(),
  Site1 = character(),
  Site2 = character(),
  PValue = numeric(),
  Regime = character()
)

genWilc_p_val = tibble(
  Analyte = character(),
  Site1 = character(),
  Site2 = character(),
  PValue = numeric()
)

genWilc_Cen_Regime = tibble(Analyte = character(),
                     FlowRegime = character(),
                     `Greenbelt Plateau` = character(),
                     `Superior Associates` = character(),
                     `Highway 36` = character(),
                     #`Dutch Creek Park` = character(),
                     `CTC Open Space` = character())#,
                     #Mulberry = character())

genWilc_Cen = tibble(Analyte = character(),
                     #FlowRegime = character(),
                     `Greenbelt Plateau` = character(),
                     `Superior Associates` = character(),
                     `Highway 36` = character(),
                     #`Dutch Creek Park` = character(),
                     `CTC Open Space` = character())


regimes = wide4 %>% pull(Type) %>% unique()
#tvd = c("Total","Filtered")

# Save just P value or not
for(pvTru in c(T,F)){
  
  for(r in regimes){
    for(c in names_censors){
      m = sub("Cen","",c)
      if(m=="Nitr"){next}
      dat = wide4 %>%
        filter(Type == r) %>%
        select(Date,Site,all_of(c(m,c))) %>%
        filter(!is.na(get(m)))
      ls = dat %>% pull(c)
      if(dat$Site %>% unique() %>% length() < 2){next}
      #    if(getPVals){
      #      if("G" %in% ls){
      #        newRow = pairwise_pVals_right(dat)
      #      } else {
      #        newRow = pairwise_pVals(dat)
      #      }
      #      genWilc_p_val = genWilc_p_val %>%
```
