```{r generalized wilcox}
      #        add_row(newRow)
      #    } else {
      if("G" %in% ls){
        newRow = pairwise_cendiff_1yr_right(dat,pvTru)
      } else {
        newRow = pairwise_cendiff_1yr(dat,pvTru)
      }
      if(pvTru){
        newRow = newRow %>%
          mutate(Analyte = m,
                 Regime = r) %>%
          select(Analyte, everything())
        
        genWilc_p_val_reg = genWilc_p_val_reg %>%
          add_row(newRow)
      } else {
        genWilc_Cen_Regime = genWilc_Cen_Regime %>%
          add_row(
            newRow %>%
              mutate(Analyte = m, FlowRegime = r))
        #Analyte = paste0(t,"_",m)
        #newRow = pairwise_cendiff_1yr(dat)
        #  genWilc_Cen_Regime = bind_rows(genWilc_Cen_Regime,newRow$Letters)
        #  genWilc_Cen_Regime$Analyte[nrow(genWilc_Cen_Regime)] = m
        #  genWilc_Cen_Regime$FlowRegime[nrow(genWilc_Cen_Regime)] = r
      }
    }
  }
  
  
  for(c in names_censors){
    m = sub("Cen","",c)
    if(m=="Nitr"){next}
    dat = wide4 %>%
      #filter(Type == r) %>%
      select(Date,Site,all_of(c(m,c))) %>%
      filter(!is.na(get(m)))
    ls = dat %>% pull(c)
    if(dat$Site %>% unique() %>% length() < 2){next}
    if("G" %in% ls){
      newRow = pairwise_cendiff_1yr_right(dat,pvTru)
    } else {
      #Analyte = paste0(t,"_",m)
      newRow = pairwise_cendiff_1yr(dat,pvTru)
    }
    if(pvTru){
      newRow = newRow %>%
        mutate(Analyte = m) %>%
        select(Analyte, everything())
      
      genWilc_p_val = genWilc_p_val %>%
        add_row(newRow)
    } else {
      genWilc_Cen = genWilc_Cen %>%
        add_row(
          newRow %>%
            mutate(Analyte = m))
      #      genWilc_Cen = bind_rows(genWilc_Cen,newRow$Letters)
      #      genWilc_Cen$Analyte[nrow(genWilc_Cen)] = m
      #    genWilc_Cen$FlowRegime[nrow(genWilc_Cen)] = r
    }
  }
  
}

# Add overall and by regime tibbles together
genWilc_All = genWilc_Cen_Regime %>%
  add_row(genWilc_Cen)

genWilc_pVals=genWilc_p_val_reg %>%
  add_row(genWilc_p_val)

#genWilc_All contains all the data for table S6
#write_csv(genWilc_All,"GeneralWilcox_All.csv")

```

```{r Manganese plot}
wilcVals = read_csv("GeneralWilcox_All.csv")
pltVar = c("MnCen_Total","MnCen_Filtered")


# Figure 8 - Set saveImg to TRUE to save the plot locally at 'pth'
regime_boxplot_many(wide4, pltVar, "Figure8", pth = "./", saveImg = F)
```

```{r Random Forest}
#Random forest related functions
one_forest = function(dataIn = wide4,
                      depVar = "AvgHBTI",
                      indeps = c(),
                      mtry = 0){
  
  dat = dataIn %>%
    filter(!is.na(Total)) %>%
    select(all_of(c(depVar,indeps))) %>%
    na.omit()
  
  results_df = dat %>%
    pull(depVar)
  
  predictors_df = dat %>%
    select(all_of(indeps))
  
  
  if(!(is.numeric(results_df))){
    results_df = factor(results_df)
  }
  
  if(mtry == 0){
    rf1 = randomForest(results_df ~ ., data = predictors_df)
  } else {
    rf1 = randomForest(results_df ~ ., data = predictors_df, mtry = mtry)
  }
  #print(depVar)
  rf1
}

# Censors data to the highest MRL in the provided set of data
highCen = function(dataInCen = data,
                   colNames = NA,
                   printPerCen = F){
  if(length(colNames) == 1){
    if(is.na(colNames)){
      colNames = names(dataInCen)
    }
  }
  dataHighCen = tibble(.rows = nrow(dataInCen))
  rowIn = nrow(dataHighCen)
  # TODO working here
# Find possible names of censor indication columns
  for(cN in colNames){
    cenName = case_when(
      grepl("_",cN) ~ gsub("_","Cen_",cN),
      T ~ paste0(cN,"Cen")
    )
    if(cenName %in% names(dataInCen)){
      cenInfo = dataInCen %>%
        select(all_of(c(cenName,cN)))
      # Find rows containing censored values
      cenRows = cenInfo %>% filter(.[[cenName]] %in% c("L","G"))
      if(nrow(cenRows) == 0){
        # No censored rows, so save the original data back, just like if no censored vals
        #print(paste0("Have a censored column for ",cN," and no points are censored."))
        dataHighCen = dataHighCen %>%
          bind_cols(dataInCen %>% select(all_of(cN)))
      } else {
        cenMax = cenRows %>% pull(cN) %>% max()
        cenOut = cenInfo %>%
          mutate({{cN}} := case_when(
            is.na(get(cN)) ~ NA,
            get(cN) < cenMax ~ cenMax,
            T ~ .[[cN]])) %>%
          select(cN)
        numCen = cenInfo %>% filter(get(cN) <= cenMax) %>% nrow()
        #print(paste0("Of the ",rowIn," samples of ",cN," there are ",
        #             numCen, " <= the max MRL of ",cenMax,"(",round(100*numCen/rowIn,1),"% censored)"))
        if(printPerCen){
          print(paste0(cN,": ",round(100*numCen/rowIn,1),"% Censored. MaxMRL: ",cenMax))
        }
        dataHighCen = dataHighCen %>%
          bind_cols(cenOut)
      }
    } else {
      # Just grab the data column since whatever the analyte is can't be censored.
      #print(paste0("The ",cN," data can't be censored."))
      dataHighCen = dataHighCen %>%
        bind_cols(dataInCen %>% select(cN))
    }
  }
  dataHighCen
}

landUse = c("BurnedStructures","ImperviousPercent",
           "BurnedWildlandPercent","BurnedUrbanPercent",
           "BurnedAcres")

names_censors = names(wide4)[grepl("Cen",names(wide4))]
names_censors_filt = names_censors[grepl("Filtered",names_censors)]
names_filt = sub("Cen","",names_censors_filt)
names_censors_tots = names_censors[grepl("Total",names_censors)]
names_censors_noMet = setdiff(names_censors,
                              c(names_censors_tots,names_censors_filt))

names_noMet = sub("Cen","",names_censors_noMet)
noMetSkip = c("Nitr","TDON","Nitrate","Nitrite","TSS",
              "Phosphate","TDP","Ammonia","Bromide","Alkalinity",
              "Temp")
noMetUse = setdiff(names_noMet,noMetSkip)

high_cen = c('Se','Cd','Tl','Cr','Co')
highC = c(
  paste0(high_cen,"_Filtered"),
  paste0(high_cen,"_Total")
)

filtUse = c(landUse,noMetUse, setdiff(names_filt,highC))
indepUse = c(filtUse,"Temp","Precip")

# original set generation, don't need to ever redo
###seeds262 = sample(1:2^12,10)
###write_csv(as_tibble(seeds262))
seeds262 = read_csv("RF_Seeds.csv") %>% pull()
nRepUse = 10
RFs = vector(mode = 'list',length = nRepUse)
MSEs = vector(mode = 'list',length = nRepUse)
VExps = vector(mode = 'list',length = nRepUse)
impsAll = tibble()
# Index of the RF to plot in text
j = 9
for(i in 1:nRepUse){
  seedNumHere = seeds262[i]
  set.seed(seedNumHere)
  #print(paste0("i : ",i," has seed num ",seedNumHere))
  indep2wk = c(indepUse[1:32],"Precip2Week")
  
  # All these are 100% censored
  newDrops = c("As_Filtered","Cu_Filtered",
               # These 3 had one measured below max MRL
               "Al_Filtered","Fe_Filtered","Pb_Filtered",
               # This had 3 points below max MRL
               "Ni_Filtered")#
  
  # Replace 100% censored Filtered values with Total values
  newAdds = setdiff(c(gsub("Filtered","Total",newDrops)),
                    # Remove As and Cu Totals since they're also 100% censored
                    c("As_Total","Cu_Total"))
  
  # Combine the add and drops from above to have a single list
  cols25 = setdiff(c("Site","Date","AvgHBTI_Scrapers",indep2wk,"Total",
                     all_of(newAdds)),
                   c("Na_Filtered","Na_Total",newDrops))
  
  # Usually and underscore means the censored column, but a few of these have '_' in
  #  the name so have to weed them out for use in censoring at highest MRL
  cols25_und = grep("_",cols25,value=T)
  cols25_noUnd = setdiff(cols25,cols25_und)
  
  cols25_undCen = gsub("_","Cen_",cols25_und)
  cols25_noUndCen = paste0(cols25_noUnd,"Cen")
  
  allCols = c(cols25,cols25_undCen,cols25_noUndCen)
  
  # The data to go into RF, but not yet censored
  rf_set2_uncen = wide4 %>% select(any_of(allCols)) %>% na.omit()
  
  # For every analyte with censored values, change any value below highest MRL
  #  to the highest MRL.
  rf_set2 = highCen(dataInCen = rf_set2_uncen,
                    colNames = cols25)

  # Make the Random Forest, without Na
  rfLoop25 = one_forest(data=rf_set2, indeps = setdiff(c(newAdds,indep2wk),
                                                       c("Na_Filtered","Na_Total",
                                                         "Temp",newDrops)),
                        depVar = "AvgHBTI_Scrapers")
  
  
  dispName = "Scrapers HBI"
  mse = round(rfLoop25$mse %>% mean(),2)
  vExp = round(rfLoop25$rsq %>% mean() * 100, 1)
  
  c1 = rfLoop25$importance %>% rownames()
  c2 = rfLoop25$importance %>% as.numeric()
  imps = tibble(var = c1, val = c2) %>% arrange(-val)
  if(nrow(impsAll) == 0){
    impsAll = imps
  } else{
    impsAll = left_join(impsAll,imps, by='var')
  }
  nc = ncol(impsAll)
  names(impsAll)[nc] = paste0("Seed:",seedNumHere)

  #varImpPlot(rfLoop25, main = paste0("Predictors for ",dispName,"\nMSE: ",mse,"\nVariance Explained: ",vExp,"%"))
  
  RFs[[i]] = rfLoop25
  MSEs[[i]] = mse
  VExps[[i]] = vExp

}

# This function doesn't save off to variable as normal. So running this will
#  display the plot and can be saved off independently.
# Figure 9
varImpPlot(RFs[[j]], main = paste0("Predictors for ",dispName,"\nMSE: ",
                                   MSEs[[j]],"\nVariance Explained: ",
                                   VExps[[j]],"%"))

# Percents sampled for table S3:
unused = highCen(dataInCen = rf_set2_uncen,
                 colNames = cols25,
                 T)

seedNums = names(impsAll)[2:11] %>%
  str_replace_all("Seed:","")

RF_Summary = tibble(rank = as.character(c(1:nrow(impsAll))))
for(i in 2:ncol(impsAll)){
  seedX = impsAll %>% select(all_of(c(1,i))) %>%
    arrange(-.[[2]]) %>%
    mutate(rank = row_number(),
           var = paste0(var,":",round(.[[2]],3)))
  names(seedX) = c(names(seedX)[2],'val','rank')
  seedX = seedX %>% select(c(1,3)) %>% mutate(rank = as.character(rank))
  RF_Summary = left_join(RF_Summary, seedX, by='rank') 
}

varExpRow = tibble(vals = c("Var Expl (%)",VExps %>% as.numeric()),
                   nams = c(1:11)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = "nams", values_from = "vals")

names(varExpRow) = names(RF_Summary)

mseRow = tibble(vals = c("MSE",MSEs %>% as.numeric()),
                nams = c(1:11)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = 'nams', values_from = 'vals')

names(mseRow) = names(RF_Summary)

RF_SummaryV = bind_rows(mseRow,varExpRow) %>%
  bind_rows(RF_Summary) %>%
  select(rank,`Seed:1827`,everything())

# Table S8
#write_csv(RF_SummaryV,"RF_10Runs.csv")
```

```{r bug summary table}
bugColNames = c("numPerSqM","Shannon Diversity Index","Species_Richness","Evenness","AvgHBI")
bugSum = wide4 %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Species_Richness)) %>%
  rename(AvgHBI = AvgHBTI) %>%
  mutate(numPerSqM = round(Total * 3.28084)) %>%
  select(Site,Date,all_of(bugColNames))

dts = bugSum$Date %>% unique()

bugSumWide = bugSum %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Date,
              values_from = all_of(bugColNames)) %>%
  mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = bugSites)) %>%
  arrange(Site)

# bugSumWide gets rearranged for table S4
```

```{r turn wide data to long for censored calculations later}
# Probably already defined, but again just in case
names_censors = names(wide4)[grepl("Cen",names(wide4))]
names_censors_filt = names_censors[grepl("Filtered",names_censors)]
names_filt = sub("Cen","",names_censors_filt)
names_censors_tots = names_censors[grepl("Total",names_censors)]
names_tots = sub("Cen","",names_censors_tots)
names_censors_noMet = setdiff(names_censors,
                              c(names_censors_tots,names_censors_filt))
names_noMet = sub("Cen","",names_censors_noMet)

names_met = c(names_filt, names_tots)

names_bugs = names(wide4)[c(3:28,178,179)]

names_land = names(wide4)[157:172]

anyCens = wide4 %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(names_censors),as.character)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(all_of(names_censors)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything()) %>%
  filter(value != "0") %>%
  pull(name) %>%
  unique()

withCensored = sub("Cen","",anyCens)

noCensoredValues = setdiff(c(names_met,names_noMet),
                          withCensored)


rightCens = wide4 %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(names_censors),as.character)) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  select(all_of(names_censors))  %>%
  pivot_longer(cols=everything()) %>%
  filter(value == "G") %>% 
  unique() %>%
  pull(name)


bothCens = wide4 %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(names_censors),as.character)) %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  select(all_of(names_censors))  %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols=everything()) %>%
  filter(value == "L") %>% 
  unique() %>%
  filter(name %in% rightCens)

#No value is both right and left censored

names_noMet = setdiff(names_noMet,"Hardness")

long_Vals = wide4 %>% 
  select("Date","Site","Type",all_of(c(names_met,names_noMet))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c("Date","Site","Type"),
               names_to = "Analyte",
               values_to = "Concentration")

long_Cen = wide4 %>% 
  mutate(across(all_of(names_censors),as.character)) %>%
  select("Date","Site",all_of(c(names_met_cen,names_noMet_cen))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c("Date","Site"),
               names_to = "AnalyteCen",
               values_to = "Censored") %>%
  mutate(Analyte = sub("Cen","",AnalyteCen),
         Censored = case_when(
           Censored == "0" ~ "FALSE",
           Censored == "L" ~ "LessThan",
           Censored == "G" ~ "GreaterThan")) %>%
  select(-AnalyteCen)

long_ValCen = full_join(long_Vals,long_Cen) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Concentration)) 

# Save off ot read in other places if needed
#write_csv(long_ValCen,"LongData.csv")
```

```{r summary statistics, warning = F}
#long_dataIn = read_csv("LongData.csv")
long_dataIn = long_ValCen
# Calculating summary statistics using different criteria based on the percentage
#  of values that are censored. 
names_basic = c("pH","Alkalinity","Conductivity","Temp")
names_nutr = c("NPOC","TDN","TDP","Ammonia","Nitrate","Phosphate")
names_anion = c("Bromide","Fluoride","Chloride","Sulfate")
names_turb = c("TSS","Turbidity")
stat_order = c(
  names_tots %>% sort(),
  names_filt %>% sort(),
  names_basic,
  names_nutr,
  names_anion,
  names_turb
)
roundSciStr = function(x=x){
  if(x<10){
    x = round(x,2)
  } else if (x<100){
    x = round(x,1)
  } else {
    x = round(x,0)
  }
  if(x>10000){
    x = format(x,big.mark=",")
  }
  as.character(x)
}
roundSci = function(x=x){
  if(x<10){
    x = round(x,2)
  } else if (x<100){
    x = round(x,1)
  } else {
    x = round(x)
  }
  x
}

right_cen = tibble(
 "Site" = character(),
 "Analyte" = character())
both_cen = tibble(
 "Site" = character(),
 "Analyte" = character())

long_data = long_dataIn %>%
  filter(!is.na(Concentration)) %>%
  rename(CenString = Censored) %>%
  mutate(Censored = CenString != "FALSE") 

# Analytes not used in summary statistics
drop_analytes = c("Nitr","Na_Filtered","Na_Total")

stat_sites = long_data$Site %>% unique()
stat_analytes = long_data$Analyte %>% unique()
stat_regimes = long_data$Type %>% unique

stat_analytes = setdiff(stat_analytes,
                        drop_analytes)

for(r in stat_regimes){
  for(s in stat_sites){
    for(a in stat_analytes){
      stat_dat = long_data %>%
        filter(Site == s, Analyte == a, Type == r)
      if(stat_dat %>% nrow() == 0){
        print(paste0("No data for ",a," at ",s,"."))
        next
      }
      if(stat_dat %>% pull(CenString) %>% unique() %>% length() > 2){
        print(paste0("Have multiple censor directions for ",
                     a," at ",s,". Look at manualy"))
        both_cen = both_cen %>% 
          add_row(tibble("Site" = s, "Analyte" = a))
        next
      }
      if(stat_dat %>% filter(CenString == "GreaterThan") %>% nrow() > 0){
        print(paste0("Have right censored data for ",
                     a," at ",s,". Look at it manually."))
        right_cen = right_cen %>%
          add_row(tibble("Site" = s, "Analyte" = a))
        next
      }
      
      maxAll = stat_dat %>% pull(Concentration) %>% max()
      minAll = stat_dat %>% pull(Concentration) %>% min()

      # Used to show what the min is below if min is below MRL
      maxMRL = stat_dat %>% filter(Censored) %>% pull(Concentration) %>% max()
      
      num = nrow(stat_dat)
      numCen = stat_dat %>% pull(Censored) %>% sum()
      perCen = numCen/num * 100
      perMeas = 100-perCen
      perMeasStr = paste0(round(perMeas,1),"%")
      # This returns K-M, then ROS, the MLE
      if(perMeas>0){
        tryCatch({
          medStats = censtats(stat_dat$Concentration,stat_dat$Censored)
        },
        error = function(e){
          medStats = ""
        })
        maxMeas = stat_dat %>% filter(!Censored) %>% pull(Concentration) %>% max()
        maxDate = stat_dat %>% filter(!Censored) %>%
          slice_max(Concentration) %>% pull(Date) %>%
          as.character()
        minMeas = stat_dat %>% filter(!Censored) %>% pull(Concentration) %>% min()
        minDate = stat_dat %>% filter(!Censored) %>%
          slice_min(Concentration) %>% pull(Date) %>%
          as.character()
        #if(maxMeas == maxAll){
        maxVal = maxMeas %>% roundSciStr() # Do this as the maximum measured
        #} else {
        #  maxVal = "<MRL"
        #}
        
        if(minMeas == minAll){
          minVal = minMeas %>% roundSciStr()
        } else {
          minVal = paste0("<",maxMRL)
#          minVal = "<MRL"
        }
        
      } else {
        maxVal = paste0("<",maxMRL)
        minVal = maxVal
#        maxVal = "<MRL"
#        minVal = "<MRL"
      }
      
      if(perMeas == 100){
        # No censored values, just calculate meian
        medVal = median(stat_dat$Concentration) %>% roundSciStr()
      } else if(perMeas > 50){
        # < 50 % censored, use Kaplan-Meier which is the 1st row of median
        medVal = medStats$median[1] %>% roundSciStr()
      } else if(perMeas >= 20){
        # 50 - 80% censored, use ROS which is the 2nd row of median
        medVal = medStats$median[2] %>% roundSciStr()
      } else {
        # > 80% censored, report percent not censored
        medVal = perMeasStr
      }
      
      if(grepl("<",minVal)){
#      if(minVal == "<MRL"){
        minDate = "NA"
      }
      if(grepl("<",maxVal)){
#      if(maxVal == "<MRL"){
        maxDate = "NA"
      }
      
      newRow = tibble(
        "Site" = s, "Analyte" = a, 
        "Min" = minVal, "Median" = medVal, "Max" = maxVal,
        "n" = num, "perMeas" = perMeasStr,
        "MinDate" = minDate, "MaxDate" = maxDate)
      
      if(which(a==stat_analytes) == 1){
        statsTib = newRow
      } else {
        statsTib = statsTib %>% add_row(newRow)
      }
      
    }
    fn = paste0(gsub(" ","",s),"_",r,"_Stats.csv")
    outTib = statsTib %>%
      filter(!is.na(Analyte)) %>%
      select(-Site)
    newnames = paste0(names(outTib)," ",gsub(" ","",s)," ",r)
    names(outTib) = newnames
    
    #write_csv(outTib,paste0("./",fn))
    
    fullTib = statsTib %>%
      mutate(FlowRegime = r) %>%
      select(Site,FlowRegime,everything())
    
    if(which(s==stat_sites) +
       which(r==stat_regimes) == 2){
      stats_long_all = fullTib
    } else {
      stats_long_all = stats_long_all %>%
        add_row(fullTib)
    }
  }
}

statsTibSave = stats_long_all %>%
  select(-c(MinDate,MaxDate)) %>%
  unique() %>%
  mutate(Analyte = factor(Analyte, levels = stat_order),
         Site = factor(Site, levels = siteOrder)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Analyte)) %>%
  arrange(Site,Analyte) %>%
  rename("% Measured" = perMeas)

statsTibWide = statsTibSave %>%
  mutate(Ste = case_when(
    Site == "Greenbelt Plateau" ~ "GBP",
    Site == "Superior Associates" ~ "SA",
    Site == "Highway 36" ~ "H36",
    Site == "Dutch Creek Park" ~ "DCP",
    Site == "CTC Open Space" ~ "CTC",
    Site == "Mulberry" ~ "MB")) %>%
  select(-Site) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Ste, values_from = -c(Ste,FlowRegime,Analyte)) %>%
  mutate(Analyte = factor(Analyte, levels = c(
    "Temp","pH","Conductivity","Alkalinity","Turbidity",
    "TDN","TDP","NPOC", "Ammonia",
    "Nitrate",
    "Chloride", "Sulfate",
    all_of(c(names_filt,names_tots))))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Analyte)) %>%
  arrange(FlowRegime,Analyte) %>%
  select(
    FlowRegime,
    Analyte,
    contains("GBP"),
    contains("SA"),
    contains("H36"),
    contains("CTC"))
    # grep("GBP",names(statsTibWide)),
    # grep("SA",names(statsTibWide)),
    # grep("H36",names(statsTibWide)),
    # grep("DCP",names(statsTibWide)),
    # grep("CTC",names(statsTibWide)),
    # grep("MB",names(statsTibWide)))


maxGreaterThans = long_dataIn %>% filter(Censored == "GreaterThan",
                                         Analyte %in% stat_analytes)
#statsTibWide
#maxGreaterThans
#  statsTibWide fills out most of table S5, with right censored values from
#   maxGreaterThans used to set the maximum values if MRL higher than max measured
```

```{r SI boxplots}
pltVar2 = c("Temp", "pH","Turbidity", "Conductivity", "Alkalinity","Hardness")
s1Plts = vector(mode = 'list',length = length(pltVar2))
for(i in 1:length(pltVar2)){
  yLab = pltVar2[i]
  Colrs4 =  c("#999999","#479f74","#56b4e9","#3172b2")
  
  pltDat = wide4 %>%
    select(Date,Site,all_of(yLab)) %>%
    mutate(Val = get(yLab)) %>%
    mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                          "Superior Associates",
                                          "Highway 36",
                                          "CTC Open Space")))
  
  pltSumry = pltDat %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    summarise(n = n(),
              min = min(get(yLab),na.rm=T),
              max = max(get(yLab),na.rm=T)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(minAll = min(min),
              maxAll = max(max))

  pltTxt = wilcVals %>% 
    filter(is.na(FlowRegime)) %>%
    filter(Analyte == yLab) %>%
    select(-c(Analyte,FlowRegime)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = 'Site', values_to = 'Let') %>%
    left_join(pltSumry) %>%
    mutate(yVal = 0-maxAll/10)
  
  plt = ggplot() +
    geom_boxplot(data = pltDat,
                 aes(x=Site, group = Site, fill = Site,
                     y = Val)) +
    bottomL +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
    labs(x = '', fill = '') +
    scale_fill_manual(values = Colrs4)
  
  if(yLab == "Conductivity"){
    plt = plt + labs(y=bquote(.(yLab)*" (μS cm"^-1*")"))
  } else if(yLab == "Temp"){
    plt = plt + labs(y = "Temp (°C)")
  } else if(yLab %in% c("Alkalinity","Hardness")){
    plt = plt+ labs(y=bquote(.(yLab)*" (mg L"^-1*" as CaCO"[3]*")"))
  } else if(yLab == "Turbidity"){
    plt = plt + labs(y = "Turbidity (NTU)")
  } else if(yLab == 'pH'){
    plt = plt + labs(y = 'pH')
  }
  
  if(yLab == 'pH'){
    plt = plt + 
      geom_text(data = pltTxt,
                aes(x = c(1:4), y = c(6,6,6,6), label = Let), size = 6) +
      geom_text(data = pltTxt,
                aes(x = c(1:4), y = c(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5), label = n),size = 6)
  } else {
    plt = plt + 
      geom_text(data = pltTxt,
                aes(x = c(1:4), y = yVal, label = Let),size = 6) + 
      geom_text(data = pltTxt,
                aes(x = c(1:4), y = yVal*1.6, label = n),size = 6)
  }
  s1Plts[[i]] = plt + theme(legend.position = 'none')
}
# Figure S2 for the SI
fig_s2 = plot_grid(plotlist = s1Plts,align = 'vh',ncol=3)


pltVar3 = c("NPOC", "TDN","Ammonia", "TDP")
s2Plts = vector(mode = 'list',length = length(pltVar3))
for(i in 1:length(pltVar3)){
  yLab = pltVar3[i]
  Colrs4 =  c("#999999","#479f74","#56b4e9","#3172b2")
  
  pltDat = wide4 %>%
    select(Date,Site,all_of(yLab)) %>%
    mutate(Val = get(yLab)) %>%
    mutate(Site = factor(Site, levels = c("Greenbelt Plateau",
                                          "Superior Associates",
                                          "Highway 36",
                                          "CTC Open Space")))
  
  pltSumry = pltDat %>%
    group_by(Site) %>%
    summarise(n = n(),
              min = min(get(yLab),na.rm=T),
              max = max(get(yLab),na.rm=T)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(minAll = min(min),
              maxAll = max(max))

  pltTxt = wilcVals %>% 
    filter(is.na(FlowRegime)) %>%
    filter(Analyte == yLab) %>%
    select(-c(Analyte,FlowRegime)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = 'Site', values_to = 'Let') %>%
    left_join(pltSumry) %>%
    mutate(yVal = 0-maxAll/15)
  
  plt = ggplot() +
    geom_boxplot(data = pltDat,
                 aes(x=Site, group = Site, fill = Site,
                     y = Val)) +
    bottomL +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
    labs(x = '', fill = '') +
    scale_fill_manual(values = Colrs4)
  
  if(yLab == "NPOC"){
    yLabUse = "DOC"
  }else if(yLab == "TDP"){
    yLabUse = "SRP"
  }else if(yLab == "Ammonia"){
    yLabUse = "Ammonium"
  } else {
    yLabUse = yLab
  }

  plt = plt + labs(y = bquote(.(yLabUse)*" (μg L"^-1*")"))
  
  
  
    plt = plt + 
      geom_text(data = pltTxt,
                aes(x = c(1:4), y = yVal*0.8, label = Let),size = 6) + 
      geom_text(data = pltTxt,
                aes(x = c(1:4), y = yVal*1.6, label = n),size = 6)
  
  s2Plts[[i]] = plt + theme(legend.position = 'none')
}

# Figure S3 from SI
fig_s3 = plot_grid(plotlist = s2Plts,align = 'vh',ncol=2)


```
